title: The  Legal Prosecution,  Judicial Conviction  and  Presidential “Absolution” of Duminda Silva.
body_lines: (This  Article was written in July 2021 following the Presidential Pardon given to Convicted Murderer Duminda Silva by President Gotabaya  Rajapaksa/It is being re-posted now in view of the historic interim order issued by the Supreme Court suspending the operation of the  Presidential Pardon)

Arumadura Lawrence Romelo Duminda Silva known as Duminda Silva was the fortunate recipient of a munificent Presidential pardon on Poson Poya day. The convict on death row was released as a result of the Poson Poya pardon.
The  Legal Prosecution,  Judicial Conviction  and  Presidential “Absolution” of Duminda Silva – by D.B.S.JEYARAJ

Duminda Silva, a former Parliamentarian and ex-Provincial Councillor, was involved in a shooting incident in October 2011 during Local Authority Elections, where former MP Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra and three of his bodyguards were killed. The High Court of Sri Lanka convicted Duminda Silva and four of his associates for murder and imposed the death sentence on them in September 2016. Subsequently the sentence was appealed but a five-Judge bench of the Supreme Court upheld the murder conviction of Silva in October 2018. Despite the High Court conviction of 2016 being upheld by the Supreme Court in 2018, Duminda Silva was a free man in 2021 on Poson Poya.

President Nandasena Gotabaya Rajapaksa invoking article 34(1) of the Constitution, granted a Presidential pardon to 93 convicted prisoners on Poson Poya day of 24 June. Of these 16 were Tamil political prisoners charged and convicted under the draconian Prevention of Terrorism Act (PTA). Of the 77 other prisoners, 76 were people who had been serving sentences for relatively minor offences. These pardons and releases (except for one) did not create any controversy. In fact the release of Tamil prisoners even earned the President some bouquets.

Murder convict Duminda Silva’s pardon and release however caused much controversy. There were many brickbats from various quarters ranging from former Justice Minister Thalatha Athukorale to US Ambassador Alaina. B. Teplitz. The Bar Association of Sri Lanka (BASL) in a detailed statement condemned the Presidential pardon for convict and former MP Duminda Silva and stated it was “unreasonable and arbitrary”.

It is against this backdrop therefore that this column focuses on the murder of Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra and the conviction of Duminda Silva. The sequence of events leading to Premachandra’s killing and the judicial proceedings in its aftermath have been written in detail by me in earlier articles. I shall rely on some of these writings as I revisit the past this week.

The shooting of Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra and conviction of Duminda Silva was a controversial episode in contemporary Sri Lankan history. Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra known as “Lucky Aiya” and three of his bodyguards were killed on 8 October 2011 in a shoot-out near the Walpola junction close to Mulleriyawa town, about six miles away from Colombo city. Another bodyguard was seriously injured.

Elections to several Local Authorities were being held on that day. Among these was the Kotikawatte-Mulleriyawa Pradeshiya Sabha. Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra had been engaged in hectic canvassing on that day when the incident occurred.

The clash was not between two rival political parties. It was between two groups of fellow United People’s Freedom Alliance (UPFA) activists. One group was led by former Colombo MP Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra. The other was led by the then Colombo District MP Duminda Silva. The Kotikawatte-Mulleriyawa Pradeshiya Sabha election was a “proxy battle” for preference votes within the UPFA between Premachandra and Silva. Bharatha backed his disciple and incumbent Chairman Prasanna Solangaarachchi. Duminda Silva worked against him and propped up another.

At the time of his death Premachandra was serving as an Adviser on Trade Unions to President Mahinda Rajapaksa. The former Colombo District MP had lost the elections of 2004 and 2010. He had been replaced as Kolonnawa Electoral Organiser by Duminda Silva who had crossed over from the UNP and been elected MP. Premachandra resented this and there was a political turf war between him and Duminda Silva. The Local Authority Poll became a prestige battle.

The campaign was nasty and marred by violence. Bharatha Lakshman with three decades of political experience turned it into a moral crusade against drugs and drug dealers. Popular antipathy towards the “kudukaraya” menace in Kolonnawa struck a responsive chord in the hearts and minds of the voters.

When results were announced it was a great political triumph for Bharatha. His “candidate” had got a record number of 42,322 preferences. His rival’s nominee had only got 5,811 preferences. The voters had overwhelmingly endorsed Bharatha Lakshman’s war against the drug mafia. In the process Premachandra had demonstrated that he was still a political force in the area. Sadly Bharatha Lakshman was not alive to savour his victory. Fate’s moving finger had written his epitaph already.

According to media reports, on Local Election day Bharatha was campaigning in one area and Solangaarachchi in another. Duminda Silva and his group campaigning in another area were reportedly in high spirits after imbibing liquor in an area called “Tamilnaduwatte”. At one stage Duminda and his accomplices went up to a polling booth in close proximity to Solangaarachchi’s residence. Duminda asked Solangaarachchi’s wife Madhu whom she voted for. There was a verbal altercation. When Madhu’s mother-in-law/Solangaarachchi’s mother remonstrated, Duminda Silva abused her also using obscene words. Some allege that both women were roughed up. Duminda also continued to intimidate and threaten Solangaarachchi’s supporters at the spot.

Bharatha Lakshman was informed of what was happening. Since he was close to the spot, Bharatha rushed to the area with his comrades. Duminda heard that Bharatha was coming and left the place. Both convoys met on the road near Walpola junction. Bharatha and Duminda both got down from their vehicles and moved towards each other.

Bharatha asked Duminda why he was trying to bully and frighten women in a cowardly manner. Duminda suddenly hit Bharatha and also kicked him. Taken by surprise Bharatha fell down. At this point Duminda ordered his men to open fire. Realising the seriousness of the situation, Bharatha’s armed “official” bodyguard also opened fire. Bharatha’s comrades who served as his unofficial bodyguards were unarmed. Bharatha and three of his comrades were shot dead. Bharatha was fired upon while he was lying on the ground. His official bodyguard too was injured.

Instead of the perpetrators being arrested, the country was regaled with what appeared to be a massive cover-up exercise. It was reported that Duminda Silva was seriously injured in the shootout. He was admitted to hospital. The then President Mahinda Rajapaksa and his Defence Secretary Gotabaya Rajapaksa visited Duminda in hospital. Special protection was provided. Outsiders were not allowed to go near Duminda. Finally he was escorted amidst great security and was flown to Singapore. It was said that he had sustained many injuries and needed extra medical treatment. Later when Duminda returned to Sri Lanka, he told authorities that he was suffering from partial memory loss and could not remember what had transpired on the day in question.
Duminda Silva was released as a result of a Presidential pardon on 24 June – pic via Daily FT

There was an inordinate delay in effective legal proceedings being instituted. There was a long period of perceived procrastination attributed euphemistically to the law’s delays. Most people however suspected that the close relationship between Duminda Silva and powerful persons in the Mahinda Rajapaksa regime was the stumbling block. The matter dragged on for years and it was widely believed that the aphorism “Justice delayed is justice denied” was being proven true in this instance also.

In an understandable yet naïve bid to secure justice while working within the ruling Rajapaksa establishment, Premachandra’s widow and daughter associated closely with President Mahinda Rajapaksa. Sumana Premachandra like her late husband was appointed as an Advisor to President Rajapaksa.

Daughter Hirunika Premachandra identified herself with the ruling Rajapaksa regime. In 2014 she contested on the UPFA ticket at elections for the Western Provincial Council. She won from Colombo District, topping the preference votes list. In spite of all these efforts the quest of justice for Bharatha seemed as elusive as ever.

Hirunika’s opportunity came when Maithripala Sirisena broke ranks with the Government in November 2014. She seized the moment by hitching her wagon to the star from Polonnaruwa. In 2015 Hirunika contested Parliamentary Elections from Colombo District on the United National Party (UNP) ticket and won. Duminda Silva however was denied nomination for polls by President Maithripala Sirisena.

The advent of a new dispensation under President Maithripala Sirisena and Prime Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe in 2015 saw a fresh breeze blowing in musty judicial corridors. The Attorney-General filed an indictment in courts on March 2015 for the killings of Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra, Dharshana Jayathilake, Mohamed Azmi and Manivel Kumaraswamy and also for inflicting gunshot injuries on Rajapurage Gamini.
The indictment consisted of 17 charges against 13 suspects including Duminda Silva.

Chief Justice Kanagasabapathy Sripavan appointed in May 2015 a three-member bench for Trial-at-Bar proceedings. The bench comprised three High Court Judges, namely Shiran Guneratne, Pathmini Ranawaka and M.C.B.S. Moraes. The Chairman was Shiran Guneratne.

Trial-at-Bar proceedings began on 22 May 2015. The case was heard on a regular basis from 12 September 2015. The names of the 13 accused were Chandana Jagath Kumara, Lanka Rasanjana, Malaka Sameera, Widanagamage Amila, Suranga Premalal, Saman Kumara, Saman Abeywickrema, Rohana Marasinghe, Duminda Silva, Anura Thushara de Mel, Chaminda Ravi Jayanath alias Dematagoda Chaminda, Dissanayake Mudiyanselage Sarath Bandara and Janaka Bandara Galagoda. The last named was tried in absentia as he was absconding. The accused were charged under Sections 296, 140, 146, 147, 486 and 300 of the Penal Code and some clauses of the Firearms Act.

Among the 17 charges were committing and conspiring to commit murder of four individuals, attempted murder, possessing illegal firearms, inflicting gunshot injuries, unlawful assembly and criminal intimidation on or around 8 October 2011. A team of lawyers from the Attorney General’s Department led by Deputy Solicitor General Thusith Mudalige represented the prosecution; 42 witnesses testified in Courts and 126 documents including Government Analysts reports and JMO reports were produced.

Trial-at-Bar proceedings concluded on 14 July 2016.The verdict in the high profile trial was delivered on 8 September 2016. It was a divided verdict with two of three Judges finding five of the accused guilty and acquitting eight others.

One judge however found all 13 accused not guilty. High Court Judge Shiran Guneratne who presided over the Trial-at-Bar exonerated all 13 accused in the murder trial, citing lack of integrity of the witnesses produced by the prosecution and the inability to prove charges beyond reasonable doubt. He said that the benefit of doubt should go to the accused. Fellow High Court Judges Pathmini Ranawaka and M.C.B.S. Moraes differed from Guneratne. This resulted in a two to one majority verdict.

High Court Judge Pathmini N. Ranawaka pronounced the two to one majority verdict in the packed Courthouse with Judge Moraes concurring. Judge Pathmini Ranawaka delivering the majority verdict stated that the prosecution had proved during the trial that the provocative conduct of 11th accused Duminda Silva led to the whole incident.

The Judge further observed that the evidence submitted has proved that Duminda Silva had provided leadership to the unruly behaviour of the mob and therefore he should be held responsible for the whole incident. She further said that the JMO reports had revealed that Duminda Silva was under the influence of liquor at the time of the incident, but deceased Bharatha Premachandra was not so. The verdict also rejected the argument put forward by the defence during trial that Silva had been shot at first by the Premachandra faction.

Courts then acquitted and discharged eight of the accused. They were – Chandana Jagath Kumara, Lanka Rasanjana, Malaka Sameera, Widanagamage Amila, Suranga Premalal, Saman Kumara, Saman Abeywickrema and Rohana Marasinghe. Death sentence was pronounced for five persons. They were – Duminda Silva, Anura Thushara de Mel, Chaminda Ravi Jayanath alias Dematagoda Chaminda, Dissanayake Mudiyanselage Sarath Bandara and Janaka Bandara Galagoda. The 10th accused Janaka Bandara Galagoda was tried in absentia and convicted. In addition to the verdict of execution, the five convicted persons were also fined Rs. 40,000 each.

The Trial-at-Bar High Court ruling was appealed at the Supreme Court. A five-Judge bench of the Supreme Court presided over by the then Chief Justice Priyasath Dep upheld the conviction unanimously. Chief Justice Dep issued a 51-page ruling on 11 October 2018. A summarised news report published on 13 October in the ‘Economy Next’ website is excerpted below:

Sri Lanka’s Supreme Court, which upheld the death penalty of MP Duminda Silva, has also highlighted his criminal activities including the use of an automatic weapon stolen from the Elephant Pass Army Camp. The outgoing Chief Justice Priyasath Dep in his 51-page order noted a string of crimes, including election law violations, committed by Silva and his cohorts culminating in the killing of Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra and three others. The five-Judge bench in its unanimous decision found that the killings came after Silva and his gang beat up rival supporters and voters at the October 2011 local council elections and had even intimidated women.

“Starting from the time the polling commenced and till the time it was drawing to an end, the 11th Accused (Duminda Silva) spent his day, marauding between polling stations with weapons, defying officials discharging their duties, and assaulting and victimising people associated with Solangaarachchi (a rival candidate).The only time they were not seen intimidating people were when the group was having lunch,” the Chief Justice said in his final order before retirement.
Justice Nalin Perera who was appointed Chief Justice on Friday, agreed with Dep’s decision. Others on the bench were justices Buwaneka Aluwihare, Priyantha Jayawardena and Vijith K. Malalgoda.

The Court held that Bharatha Lakshman and three others were shot dead at the behest of Duminda Silva and the weapon used had been stolen from the Elephant Pass Military Camp when it was overrun by the Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam (LTTE) in 2000. It was not mentioned how Silva, a Monitoring MP of the Defence Ministry at the time, came to possess this weapon.

The Supreme Court rejected Silva’s position that he had been shot in the head by Premachandra’s officially-assigned bodyguard and was not responsible for subsequent events, including the killings of Premachandra and the other three people. However, the Court rejected this position on the basis that Silva had been leading the unlawful assembly and triggered the violence by first assaulting Premachandra, whose Personal Security Officer, using the right to private defence, then opened fire and injured Silva.

“Up to the very minute he was shot in his head, the 11th Accused (Duminda Silva) was leading the unlawful assembly. This means that there could only have been a millisecond difference in time between the first shot and the retaliation.

“Causing death using firearms was very much a foreseeable consequence of their criminal enterprise. It is also true that the 11th Accused (Duminda Silva) was a member of that assembly when the transaction—which lasted for a fleeting 60 or more seconds—commenced.

“At the same time, there is no evidence to suggest that, at any time prior to that, the 11th Accused (Duminda Silva) showed a tendency to disassociate himself with the object of the assembly,” the judgement said.

However, the Court acquitted Duminda Silva’s official bodyguard, constable Vithanalage Anura Thushara de Mel who had not used a weapon and had also been against getting into a gun battle. The Supreme Court held that the prosecution had failed to establish that de Mel shared the common objective of the unlawful assembly and that he should therefore be acquitted.

Duminda Silva’s gang had used only one T56 weapon, the one stolen from the Elephant Pass Camp, and 27 spent cartridges found at the crime scene had been fired from it. The Court found that Duminda Silva had systematically moved from one polling centre to the other intimidating voters and had assaulted voters they suspected of voting for the opposition. Women were intimidated and there was a string of violence against people in the area.

This then was what happened in the prosecution and conviction of Duminda Silva for the murder of Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra. Now Duminda has been released due to the clemency of President Rajapaksa. No explanation has been given. There was no reference to the Presidential pardon in the President’s 70-minute televised address to the nation on 25 June.
Under these circumstances, the Bar Association of Sri Lanka BASL has written to the President and requested that the President to the BASL and to the general public:
(a) The basis on which Duminda Silva was selected for the purpose of granting a pardon under Article 34 (1) of the Constitution;
(b) The circumstances which were taken into consideration in the granting of such pardon;
(c) The reasons as to why the case of Duminda Silva stands out from others who are currently sentenced;

(d) Whether a report was called for by His Excellency the President from the Trial Judges as required by the Proviso to Article 34 (1) prior to granting of the pardon to Duminda Silva and if so the contents of the report;
(e) Whether the advice of the Attorney General was called for prior to granting of the pardon to Duminda Silva and if so the contents of such advice;
(f) Whether the recommendation of the Minister of Justice was obtained prior to granting of the pardon to Duminda Silva and if so whether the Minister of Justice made such a recommendation;

“The Bar Association of Sri Lanka maintains that if any one or more considerations stated above, were not satisfied in the current case, the pardon granted to Duminda Silva would be unreasonable and arbitrary and will result in erosion to the rule of law and result in a loss of public confidence in respect of the administration of justice,” it pointed out.
This Article appears in the “Political Pulse”Column of the “Daily FT” dated June 30th 2021.It can be accessed here:

si.title: දුමින්ද සිල්වාගේ නීතිමය නඩු පැවරීම, අධිකරණමය වරදකරු කිරීම සහ ජනාධිපති "නිදහස".
body_lines: (මෙම ලිපිය ලියා ඇත්තේ 2021 ජූලි මාසයේදී වැරදිකරු වූ මිනීමරු දුමින්ද සිල්වාට ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ විසින් ලබා දුන් ජනාධිපති සමාවෙන් පසුවය / ජනාධිපති සමාව ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීම අත්හිටුවමින් ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණය විසින් නිකුත් කරන ලද ඓතිහාසික අතුරු නියෝගය සැලකිල්ලට ගනිමින් එය දැන් නැවත පළ කරනු ලැබේ)

දුමින්ද සිල්වා නමින් හැඳින්වෙන අරුමදුර ලෝරන්ස් රොමෙලෝ දුමින්ද සිල්වා මහතා පොසොන් පොහෝ දින ජනාධිපති සමාව ලැබූ භාග්‍යවන්තයාය. පොසොන් පොහොය සමාව ප්‍රතිඵලයක් ලෙස මරණ දණ්ඩනයට නියම වී සිටි සිරකරු නිදහස් විය.
දුමින්ද සිල්වාගේ නීත්‍යානුකූල නඩු පැවරීම, අධිකරණමය වරදකරු කිරීම සහ ජනාධිපති "නිදහස" - D.B.S.JEYARAJ විසිනි

හිටපු පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රීවරයෙකු සහ හිටපු පළාත් සභා මන්ත්‍රීවරයෙකු වන දුමින්ද සිල්වා 2011 වසරේ ඔක්තෝම්බර් මාසයේදී පළාත් පාලන මැතිවරණය අතරතුර වෙඩි තැබීමේ සිද්ධියකට සම්බන්ධ වූ අතර එහිදී හිටපු පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රී භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර සහ ඔහුගේ ආරක්ෂකයින් තිදෙනෙකු ජීවිතක්ෂයට පත්වුණා. ශ්‍රී ලංකා මහාධිකරණය දුමින්ද සිල්වා සහ ඔහුගේ සගයන් සිව් දෙනෙකු මිනීමැරුම් චෝදනාවට වරදකරු කර 2016 සැප්තැම්බර් මාසයේදී ඔවුන්ට මරණ දඬුවම නියම කළේය. පසුව එම දඬුවම අභියාචනය කරන ලද නමුත් ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණයේ පංච පුද්ගල විනිසුරු මඩුල්ලක් විසින් ඔක්තෝම්බර් මාසයේදී සිල්වා ඝාතනය කිරීමේ වරද තහවුරු කරන ලදී. 2018. 2016 මහාධිකරණයේ වරද 2018 දී ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණය විසින් තහවුරු කරනු ලැබුවද දුමින්ද සිල්වා 2021 පොසොන් පෝදා නිදහස් මිනිසෙකි.

ජනාධිපති නන්දසේන ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ මහතා ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාවේ 34(1) වගන්තිය ක්‍රියාවට නංවමින් වරදකරුවන් වූ සිරකරුවන් 93 දෙනෙකුට ජුනි 24 පොසොන් පොහොය දිනයේදී ජනාධිපති සමාව ලබා දුන්නේය. මෙම 16 දෙනාගෙන් දෙමළ දේශපාලන සිරකරුවන් වූයේ කුරිරු ත්‍රස්තවාදය වැළැක්වීමේ පනත (PTA) යටතේ චෝදනා ලැබ වරදකරුවන් වූ අයයි. අනෙකුත් සිරකරුවන් 77 දෙනාගෙන් 76 දෙනෙකුම සාපේක්ෂව සුළු වැරදි සඳහා සිරදඬුවම් විඳිමින් සිටි අයයි. මෙම සමාව සහ නිදහස් කිරීම් (එකක් හැර) කිසිදු මතභේදයක් ඇති කළේ නැත. ඇත්තටම දෙමළ සිරකරුවන් නිදහස් කිරීම නිසා ජනාධිපතිතුමාට මල් පොකුර පවා ලැබුණා.

කෙසේ වෙතත් ඝාතන චූදිත දුමින්ද සිල්වා සමාව දී නිදහස් කිරීම මහත් ආන්දෝලනයකට තුඩු දුන්නේය. හිටපු අධිකරණ ඇමැතිනී තලතා අතුකෝරළ මහත්මියගේ සිට ඇමෙරිකානු තානාපතිනී ඇලෙයිනා දක්වා විවිධ පාර්ශ්වවලින් ගඩොල් බැට්‌ටියෝ රැසක්‌ සිටියහ. බී. ටෙප්ලිට්ස්. ශ්‍රී ලංකා නීතීඥ සංගමය (BASL) සවිස්තරාත්මක ප්‍රකාශයක් කරමින් වැරදිකරු සහ හිටපු පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රී දුමින්ද සිල්වාට ජනාධිපති සමාව ලබා දීම හෙළා දකින අතර එය "අසාධාරණ සහ අත්තනෝමතික" බව ප්‍රකාශ කළේය.

භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර ඝාතනය සහ දුමින්ද සිල්වා වරදකරු කිරීම සම්බන්ධයෙන් මෙම තීරුව අවධානය යොමු කරන්නේ මෙවැනි පසුබිමක ය. ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර ඝාතනයට තුඩු දුන් සිදුවීම් පෙළ සහ ඉන් පසුව සිදු වූ අධිකරණ ක්‍රියාදාමයන් මා විසින් මීට පෙර ලිපිවල සවිස්තරාත්මකව ලියා ඇත. මම මේ සතියේ අතීතය නැවත සලකා බලන විට මෙම ලියවිලිවලින් සමහරක් මත විශ්වාසය තබමි.

භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර මහතාට වෙඩි තබා දුමින්ද සිල්වා වරදකරු කිරීම සමකාලීන ශ්‍රී ලංකා ඉතිහාසයේ ආන්දෝලනාත්මක සිදුවීමක් විය. කොළඹ නගරයට සැතපුම් හයක් පමණ දුරින් මුල්ලේරියාව නගරයට නුදුරින් වල්පොල හන්දිය අසල ඇති වූ වෙඩි හුවමාරුවකදී 2011 ඔක්තෝම්බර් 8 වැනි දින “ලකී අයියා” ලෙසින් හැඳින්වූ භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර සහ ඔහුගේ ආරක්ෂකයින් තිදෙනෙකු ඝාතනය විය. තවත් ආරක්ෂකයෙකු බරපතල තුවාල ලබා ඇත.
එදින පළාත් පාලන ආයතන කිහිපයක මැතිවරණ පැවැත්වෙමින් තිබිණි. මේ අතර කොටිකාවත්ත - මුල්ලේරියාව ප්‍රාදේශීය සභාව ද විය. සිද්ධිය වූ එදින භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර මහතා දැඩි කැන්වසින් කටයුතුවල නිරතව සිට ඇත.

ගැටුම ඇතිවූයේ ප්‍රතිවාදී දේශපාලන පක්ෂ දෙකක් අතර නොවේ. ඒ එක්සත් ජනතා නිදහස් සන්ධානයේ සහෝදර ක්‍රියාකාරීන් දෙපිරිසක් අතරයි. එක් කණ්ඩායමකට නායකත්වය දුන්නේ කොළඹ හිටපු පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රී භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර. අනෙකට නායකත්වය දුන්නේ එවකට කොළඹ දිස්ත්‍රික් පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රී දුමින්ද සිල්වාය. කොටිකාවත්ත - මුල්ලේරියාව ප්‍රාදේශීය සභා මැතිවරණය ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර සහ සිල්වා අතර එක්සත් ජනතා නිදහස් සන්ධානය තුළ මනාප සඳහා “ප්‍රොක්සි සටනක්” විය. භාරත තම ගෝලයා සහ වත්මන් සභාපති ප්‍රසන්න සෝලංගආරච්චිට සහාය දුන්නේය. දුමින්ද සිල්වා ඔහුට විරුද්ධව වැඩ කර තවකෙකුට මුක්කු ගැසුවේය.

මියයන විට ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර මහතා ජනාධිපති මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ මහතාගේ වෘත්තීය සමිති පිළිබඳ උපදේශකවරයකු ලෙස සේවය කරමින් සිටියේය. කොළඹ දිස්ත්‍රික් හිටපු මන්ත්‍රීවරයා 2004 සහ 2010 මැතිවරණවලින් පරාජයට පත් වූ අතර ඔහු වෙනුවට කොළොන්නාව ආසන සංවිධායක ලෙස පත් කර තිබුණේ එක්සත් ජාතික පක්ෂයෙන් පිටව ගොස් මන්ත්‍රීවරයා ලෙස තේරී පත් වූ දුමින්ද සිල්වා මහතාය. ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර මේ ගැන අමනාප වූ අතර ඔහු සහ දුමින්ද සිල්වා අතර දේශපාලන තණතිලි යුද්ධයක් ඇති විය. පළාත් පාලන ඡන්ද විමසීම කීර්තිමත් සටනක් බවට පත් විය.

ව්‍යාපාරය නපුරු වූ අතර ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වයෙන් විනාශ විය. දශක තුනක දේශපාලන අත්දැකීම් ඇති භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් එය මත්ද්‍රව්‍ය හා මත්ද්‍රව්‍ය ජාවාරම්කරුවන්ට එරෙහි සදාචාරාත්මක සටනක් බවට පත් කළේය. කොළොන්නාවේ "කුඩුකාරයා" උවදුරට එරෙහි ජනප්‍රිය විරෝධය ඡන්දදායකයින්ගේ හදවත් හා මනස්වලට ප්‍රතිචාරාත්මක ස්වරයකින් පහර දුන්නේය.

ප්‍රතිඵල නිකුත් වන විට භාරත ලැබූ විශිෂ්ට දේශපාලන ජයග්‍රහණයකි. ඔහුගේ “අපේක්‍ෂකයාට” වාර්තාගත මනාප 42,322ක් ලැබී තිබුණි. ඔහුගේ ප්‍රතිවාදියාගේ නාමිකයාට ලැබී තිබුණේ මනාප 5,811ක් පමණි. මත්ද්‍රව්‍ය මාෆියාවට එරෙහි භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන්ගේ යුද්ධය ඡන්දදායකයින් වැඩි වශයෙන් අනුමත කර තිබුණි. එම ක්‍රියාවලියේදී ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර තමන් තවමත් ප්‍රදේශයේ දේශපාලන බලවේගයක් බව පෙන්නුම් කර තිබුණි. කනගාටුදායක ලෙස භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ඔහුගේ ජයග්‍රහණය රසවිඳීමට ජීවතුන් අතර සිටියේ නැත. දෛවයේ චලනය වන ඇඟිල්ල දැනටමත් ඔහුගේ අභිලේඛනය ලියා ඇත.

මාධ්‍ය වාර්තාවලට අනුව, පළාත් පාලන මැතිවරණ දිනයේ භාරත එක් ප්‍රදේශයක ද සෝලංගආරච්චි තවත් ප්‍රදේශයක ද ප්‍රචාරක කටයුතුවල නිරත විය. තවත් ප්‍රදේශයක මැතිවරණ ප්‍රචාරක කටයුතුවල නිරතව සිටි දුමින්ද සිල්වා ඇතුළු පිරිස “තමිල්නාදුවත්ත” නම් ප්‍රදේශයේ මත්පැන් පානය කිරීමෙන් පසුව දැඩි උද්යෝගයෙන් සිටි බව වාර්තා වේ. එක් අවස්ථාවක දුමින්ද සහ ඔහුගේ සගයන් සෝලංගආරච්චිගේ නිවසට ආසන්නයේ පිහිටි ඡන්ද මධ්‍යස්ථානයකට නැග්ගා. දුමින්ද සෝලංගආරච්චිගේ බිරිද මධුගෙන් ඇහුවා කාටද ඡන්දය දුන්නේ කියලා. එහිදී වාචික ගැටුමක් ඇති විය. මධුගේ නැන්දම්මා / සෝලංගආරච්චිගේ මව විරෝධය පළ කළ විට දුමින්ද සිල්වා ඇයට ද අසභ්‍ය වචන භාවිත කළේය. සමහරු චෝදනා කරන්නේ කාන්තාවන් දෙදෙනාම රළු වූ බවයි. දුමින්ද එම ස්ථානයේ සිටි සෝලංගආරච්චිගේ ආධාරකරුවන්ට බිය ගැන්වීම සහ තර්ජනය කිරීමද දිගින් දිගටම සිදු කළේය.

සිදුවන්නේ කුමක්ද යන්න භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් මහතාට දැනුම් දෙන ලදී. ඔහු එම ස්ථානයට ආසන්නව සිටි බැවින් භාරත තම සගයන් සමඟ එම ප්‍රදේශයට දිව ගියේය. භාරත එන බව දැනගත් දුමින්ද එම ස්ථානයෙන් පිටව ගියේය. රථ පෙළ දෙකම වල්පොල හන්දිය අසල පාරේදී හමු විය. භාරත සහ දුමින්ද දෙදෙනාම වාහනවලින් බැස එකිනෙකා දෙසට ගමන් කළහ.

භාරත දුමින්දගෙන් ඇසුවේ බියගුලු ලෙස කාන්තාවන්ට හිරිහැර කිරීමට හා බිය ගැන්වීමට උත්සාහ කරන්නේ මන්ද යන්නයි. දුමින්ද එකපාරටම භාරතට ගහලා පයින් ගැහුවා. පුදුමයෙන් භාරත ඇද වැටුණේය. මේ අවස්ථාවේදී දුමින්ද තම පිරිසට වෙඩි තියන්නට නියෝග කළේය. තත්ත්වය බරපතළ බව වටහාගත් භාරතගේ සන්නද්ධ “නිල” ආරක්ෂකයාද වෙඩි තැබීය. භාරතගේ නිල නොලත් ආරක්ෂකයින් ලෙස කටයුතු කළ ඔහුගේ සහෝදරයෝ නිරායුධව සිටියහ. භාරත සහ ඔහුගේ සගයන් තිදෙනෙකු වෙඩි තබා ඝාතනය කරන ලදී. භාරත බිම වැතිර සිටියදී වෙඩි තබා ඇත. ඔහුගේ නිල ආරක්ෂකයාද තුවාල ලබා ඇත.

අපරාධකරුවන් අත්අඩංගුවට ගැනීම වෙනුවට, දැවැන්ත වසන් කිරීමේ ව්‍යායාමයක් ලෙස පෙනෙන දෙයකින් රට පාලනය කරන ලදී. වෙඩි හුවමාරුවෙන් දුමින්ද සිල්වා බරපතල තුවාල ලබා ඇති බවයි වාර්තා වුණේ. ඔහු රෝහලට ඇතුළත් කළා. දුමින්ද බැලීමට එවකට ජනාධිපති මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ මහතා සහ ඔහුගේ ආරක්ෂක ලේකම් ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ මහතා රෝහලට ගියහ. විශේෂ ආරක්ෂාවක් සපයා තිබුණි. දුමින්ද ළඟට පිටස්තර අයට යන්න දුන්නේ නැහැ. අවසානයේ දැඩි ආරක්‍ෂාව මධ්‍යයේ ඔහුව රැගෙන ගොස් සිංගප්පූරුව බලා පිටත් කර යවන ලදී. ඔහුට තුවාල රැසක් සිදුවී ඇති අතර අමතර වෛද්‍ය ප්‍රතිකාර අවශ්‍ය බවද පැවසේ. පසුව දුමින්ද ශ්‍රී ලංකාවට පැමිණි විට බලධාරීන්ට පවසා ඇත්තේ තමාට මතකය අර්ධ වශයෙන් අහිමි වී ඇති බවත් අදාළ දිනයේ සිදුවූයේ කුමක්ද යන්න මතක නැති බවත්ය.
ජූනි 24 වෙනිදා ජනාධිපති සමාවේ ප්‍රතිඵලයක් ලෙස දුමින්ද සිල්වා නිදහස් විය - ඡායාරූපය Daily FT

ඵලදායි නීතිමය ක්‍රියාදාමයන් ආරම්භ කිරීමේදී අප්‍රමාණ ප්‍රමාදයක් ඇති විය. නීතියේ ප්‍රමාදයන් සඳහා සුහදශීලී ලෙස ආරෝපණය කරන ලද දිගු කාලයක් කල්දැමීමක් සිදු විය. කෙසේ වෙතත් දුමින්ද සිල්වා සහ මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ පාලනයේ ප්‍රබලයින් අතර පැවති සමීප සබඳතාව ඊට බාධාවක් බවට බොහෝ දෙනා සැක පළ කළා. මෙම කාරණය වසර ගණනාවක් ඇදී ගිය අතර, "යුක්තිය ප්‍රමාද වූ විට යුක්තිය ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කිරීම" යන පුරාවෘත්තය මෙම අවස්ථාවේදීද සත්‍ය බව බොහෝ දෙනා විශ්වාස කළහ.

පාලක රාජපක්ෂ සංස්ථාපිතය තුළ වැඩ කරන අතරතුර යුක්තිය තහවුරු කර ගැනීමේ තේරුම්ගත හැකි නමුත් බොළඳ උත්සාහයක් තුළ, ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍රගේ වැන්දඹු බිරිඳ සහ දියණිය ජනාධිපති මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ සමඟ සමීපව ඇසුරු කළහ. සුමනා ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර මැතිනියගේ නැසීගිය ස්වාමිපුරුෂයා මෙන්ම ජනාධිපති රාජපක්ෂගේ උපදේශකවරියක් ලෙස පත් කරන ලදී.

දියණිය හිරුණිකා ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර තමන්ව හඳුනාගෙන ඇත්තේ පාලක රාජපක්ෂ පාලනය සමඟයි. 2014 දී ඇය බස්නාහිර පළාත් සභා මැතිවරණයට එක්සත් ජනතා නිදහස් සන්ධානයෙන් තරග කළාය. ඇය මනාප ලැයිස්තුවේ ඉහළින්ම කොළඹ දිස්ත්‍රික්කයෙන් ජයග්‍රහණය කළාය. මේ සියලු උත්සාහයන් නොතකා භාරත වෙනුවෙන් යුක්තිය සොයා යාම වෙනදා මෙන් නොපැහැදිලි විය.

2014 නොවැම්බරයේ මෛත්‍රීපාල සිරිසේන මහතා රජය සමඟ කැඩිලා ගිය අවස්ථාවේදී හිරුණිකාට අවස්ථාව උදා විය. ඇය එම අවස්ථාව ලබා ගත්තේ පොළොන්නරුවේ සිට තම කරත්තය තරුවට නංවාගෙනය. 2015 දී හිරුණිකා එක්සත් ජාතික පක්ෂයෙන් (එජාප) කොළඹ දිස්ත්‍රික්කයෙන් පාර්ලිමේන්තු මැතිවරණයට ඉදිරිපත් වී ජයග්‍රහණය කළාය. කෙසේ වෙතත්, ජනාධිපති මෛත්‍රීපාල සිරිසේන මහතා විසින් දුමින්ද සිල්වා මහතාට මැතිවරණය සඳහා නාමයෝජනා දීම ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කරන ලදී.

2015 දී ජනාධිපති මෛත්‍රීපාල සිරිසේන සහ අගමැති රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ යටතේ නව රජයක් පැමිණීමත් සමඟම අධිකරණ මංසන්ධිවල නැවුම් සුළඟක් හමා ගියේය. භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර, දර්ශන ජයතිලක, මොහොමඩ් අස්මි සහ මනිවේල් කුමාරස්වාමි යන අයගේ ඝාතන සම්බන්ධයෙන් සහ රාජපුරගේ ගාමිණීට වෙඩි වැදී තුවාල සිදුකිරීම සම්බන්ධයෙන් නීතිපතිවරයා 2015 මාර්තු මාසයේදී අධිකරණයට අධි චෝදනා ගොනු කළේය.
දුමින්ද සිල්වා ඇතුළු විත්තිකරුවන් 13 දෙනෙකුට එරෙහිව චෝදනා 17 කින් මෙම අධි චෝදනා පත්‍රය සමන්විත විය.

අගවිනිසුරු කනගසබාපති ශ්‍රීපවන් විසින් 2015 මැයි මාසයේදී නඩු විභාගය සඳහා ත්‍රිපුද්ගල විනිසුරු මඩුල්ලක් පත් කරන ලදී. ශිරාන් ගුණරත්න, පත්මිණි රණවක සහ එම්.සී.බී.එස් යන මහාධිකරණ විනිසුරුවරුන් තිදෙනාගෙන් මෙම විනිසුරු මඩුල්ල සමන්විත විය. මොරායස්. සභාපතිවරයා වූයේ ශිරාන් ගුණරත්නය.

2015 මැයි 22 දින නඩු විභාගය ආරම්භ විය. මෙම නඩුව 2015 සැප්තැම්බර් 12 දින සිට නිතිපතා විභාගයට ගන්නා ලදී. විත්තිකරුවන් 13 දෙනාගේ නම් චන්දන ජගත් කුමාර, ලංකා රසාංජන, මාලක සමීර, විදානගමගේ අමිල, සුරංග ප්‍රේමලාල්, සමන් කුමාර , සමන් අබේවික්‍රම, රෝහණ මාරසිංහ, දුමින්ද සිල්වා, අනුර තුෂාර ද මෙල්, චමින්ද රවී ජයනාත් නොහොත් දෙමටගොඩ චමින්ද, දිසානායක මුදියන්සේලාගේ සරත් බණ්ඩාර සහ ජනක බණ්ඩාර ගලගොඩ. අන්තිමට නම් කරන ලද පුද්ගලයා සැඟවී සිටි බැවින් ඔහු නොපැමිණීමේදී නඩු විභාගයට ලක් විය. දණ්ඩ නීති සංග්‍රහයේ 296, 140, 146, 147, 486 සහ 300 වගන්ති සහ ගිනි අවි පනතේ වගන්ති කිහිපයක් යටතේ විත්තිකරුවන්ට චෝදනා එල්ල විය.

2011 ඔක්‌තෝබර් 8 වැනි දින හෝ ඊට ආසන්න දිනකදී පුද්ගලයන් සිවු දෙනකු ඝාතනය කිරීම, ඝාතනය කිරීමට කුමන්ත්‍රණය කිරීම, මිනීමැරීමට තැත් කිරීම, නීතිවිරෝධී ගිනි අවි ළඟ තබා ගැනීම, වෙඩි තැබීම්වලින් තුවාල සිදුකිරීම, නීති විරෝධී රැස්වීම් සහ සාපරාධී බිය ගැන්වීම් ඇතුළු චෝදනා 17ක් ඇතුළත් විය. පැමිණිල්ල වෙනුවෙන් පෙනී සිටි සොලිසිටර් ජනරාල් තුසිත් මුදලිගේ මහතා; සාක්‍ෂිකරුවන් 42 දෙනෙකු අධිකරණයේ සාක්‍ෂි දුන් අතර රජයේ රස පරීක්‍ෂකවරුන්ගේ වාර්තා, අධිකරණ වෛද්‍ය නිලධාරී වාර්තා ඇතුළු ලේඛන 126 ක්‌ ඉදිරිපත් කෙරිණි.

2016 ජූලි 14 වැනි දින නඩු විභාගය අවසන් විය. ඉහළ පෙළේ නඩු විභාගයේ තීන්දුව 2016 සැප්තැම්බර් 8 වැනි දින ප්‍රකාශයට පත් කරන ලදී. එය විනිසුරුවරුන් තිදෙනෙකුගෙන් දෙදෙනකු විත්තිකරුවන් පස් දෙනෙකු වැරදිකරුවන් බවට පත් කර තවත් අට දෙනෙකු නිදොස් කොට නිදහස් කරමින් බෙදී ගිය තීන්දුවක් විය.

කෙසේ වෙතත් එක් විනිසුරුවරයෙකු විත්තිකරුවන් 13 දෙනාම නිවැරැදිකරුවන් බව තීන්දු කළේය. පැමිණිල්ල විසින් ඉදිරිපත් කරන ලද සාක්ෂිකරුවන්ගේ අවංකභාවය නොමැතිකම සහ සාධාරණ සැකයෙන් ඔබ්බට චෝදනා ඔප්පු කිරීමට නොහැකි වීම යන කරුණු පවසමින් ඝාතන නඩුවේ විත්තිකරුවන් 13 දෙනාම නිදොස් කොට නිදහස් කරන ලදී. සැකයේ වාසිය විත්තිකරුට හිමිවිය යුතු බවද ඔහු පැවසීය. සෙසු මහාධිකරණ විනිසුරුවරුන් වන පත්මිණී රණවක සහ එම්.සී.බී.එස්. මොරායස් ගුණරත්නගෙන් වෙනස් විය. එහි ප්‍රතිඵලය වූයේ බහුතර දෙකට එකක් බහුතර තීන්දුවකි.

මහාධිකරණ විනිසුරු පත්මිණී එන්. රණවක විනිසුරු මොරායස්ගේ එකඟතාවයෙන් පිරී ගිය අධිකරණ ශාලාවේදී බහුතර තීන්දුවක් දෙකට ප්‍රකාශ කළාය. 11 වැනි විත්තිකාර දුමින්ද සිල්වාගේ ප්‍රකෝපකාරී ක්‍රියාකලාපය සමස්ත සිද්ධියට හේතු වූ බව නඩු විභාගයේදී පැමිණිල්ල විසින් ඔප්පු කර ඇති බව විනිසුරු පත්මිණි රණවක මහත්මිය බහුතර තීන්දුව ප්‍රකාශයට පත් කරමින් ප්‍රකාශ කළාය.

මෙම මැර කල්ලියේ නොසන්සුන්කාරී හැසිරීමට නායකත්වය දුමින්ද සිල්වා විසින් ලබාදී ඇති බවට ඉදිරිපත් වූ සාක්ෂිවලින් ඔප්පු වී ඇති බවත්, එබැවින් ඔහු සම්පූර්ණ සිද්ධියට වගකිව යුතු බවත් විනිසුරුවරයා වැඩිදුරටත් සඳහන් කළේය. සිද්ධිය වන අවස්ථාවේ දුමින්ද සිල්වා බීමතින් සිට ඇති බව අධිකරණ වෛද්‍ය නිලධාරීවරයාගේ වාර්තාවලින් හෙළි වූ නමුත් මියගිය භාරත ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර එසේ නොසිටි බවද ඇය පැවසුවාය. ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර පාර්ශ්වයෙන් ප්‍රථමයෙන් සිල්වාට වෙඩි තැබූ බවට නඩු විභාගයේදී විත්ති පාර්ශ්වය ඉදිරිපත් කළ තර්කය ද තීන්දුවෙන් ප්‍රතික්ෂේප විය.

ඉන්පසුව අධිකරණය විත්තිකරුවන් අට දෙනෙකු නිදොස් කොට නිදහස් කළේය. ඒ - චන්දන ජගත් කුමාර, ලංකා රසාංජන, මාලක සමීර, විදානගමගේ අමිල, සුරංග ප්‍රේමලාල්, සමන් කුමාර, සමන් අබේවික්‍රම සහ රෝහණ මාරසිංහ. පස් දෙනෙකුට මරණ දඬුවම නියම විය. ඒ දුමින්ද සිල්වා, අනුර තුෂාර ද මෙල්, චමින්ද රවී ජයනාත් නොහොත් දෙමටගොඩ චමින්ද, දිසානායක මුදියන්සේලාගේ සරත් බණ්ඩාර සහ ජනක බණ්ඩාර ගලගොඩ යන අයයි. 10 වැනි විත්තිකරු ජනක බණ්ඩාර ගලගොඩ මහතා නොපැමිණීම සම්බන්ධයෙන් නඩු විභාගයක් පවත්වා වරදකරු කරනු ලැබීය. මරණීය දණ්ඩනය ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීමේ තීන්දුවට අමතරව වරදකරුවන් වූ පස්දෙනාට රු. 40,000 බැගින්.

මහාධිකරණ තීන්දුවට එරෙහිව ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණයේ අභියාචනයක් ඉදිරිපත් විය. එවකට අගවිනිසුරු ප්‍රියසාත් ඩෙප්ගේ ප්‍රධානත්වයෙන් යුත් පංච පුද්ගල ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණ විනිසුරු මඩුල්ලක් විසින් එම වරද ඒකමතිකව තහවුරු කරන ලදී. අගවිනිසුරු ඩෙප් 2018 ඔක්තෝබර් 11 වැනි දින පිටු 51කින් යුත් තීන්දුවක් නිකුත් කළේය. ඔක්තෝබර් 13 වැනි දින ‘Economy Next’ වෙබ් අඩවියේ පළ වූ සාරාංශගත පුවත් වාර්තාවක් පහතින් උපුටා දක්වා ඇත.

පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රී දුමින්ද සිල්වාගේ මරණීය දණ්ඩනය තහවුරු කළ ශ්‍රී ලංකා ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණය අලිමංකඩ හමුදා කඳවුරෙන් සොරකම් කරන ලද ස්වයංක්‍රීය ගිනි අවියක් භාවිත කිරීම ඇතුළු ඔහුගේ අපරාධ ක්‍රියා ද ඉස්මතු කර තිබේ. ඉවත්ව යන අගවිනිසුරු ප්‍රියසාත් ඩෙප් සිය පිටු 51 කින් යුත් නියෝගයෙන් සිල්වා සහ ඔහුගේ සහචරයින් විසින් සිදු කරන ලද මැතිවරණ නීති උල්ලංඝණය කිරීම් ඇතුළු අපරාධ වැලක් භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර සහ තවත් තිදෙනෙකු ඝාතනය කිරීම දක්වා ගෙන ගියේය. පංච පුද්ගල විනිසුරු මඩුල්ල එහි ඒකමතික තීන්දුවෙන් සොයා ගත්තේ සිල්වා සහ ඔහුගේ කල්ලිය 2011 ඔක්තෝම්බර් ප්‍රාදේශීය සභා මැතිවරණයේදී ප්‍රතිවාදී ආධාරකරුවන්ට සහ ඡන්දදායකයින්ට පහර දී කාන්තාවන් බිය ගැන්වීමෙන් පසුව මෙම ඝාතන සිදු වූ බවයි.

“ඡන්දය ආරම්භ වූ මොහොතේ සිට එය අවසන් වන තුරුම 11 වැනි විත්තිකරු (දුමින්ද සිල්වා) දවස ගෙවා දැමුවේ ආයුධ අතැතිව ඡන්ද මධ්‍යස්ථාන අතර මංකොල්ලකෑම, රාජකාරියට නොපැමිණෙන නිලධාරීන්ට පහර දීම සහ සම්බන්ධ පුද්ගලයන්ට පහර දී ඝාතනය කිරීම ය. සෝලංගආරච්චි (ප්‍රතිවාදී අපේක්ෂකයෙක්) ඔවුන් මිනිසුන් බිය ගන්වමින් සිටිනු නොපෙනී ගිය එකම අවස්ථාව වූයේ කණ්ඩායම දිවා ආහාරය ගනිමින් සිටින විටයි,” අගවිනිසුරුවරයා විශ්‍රාම යාමට පෙර සිය අවසන් නියෝගයේ සඳහන් කළේය.
සිකුරාදා අගවිනිසුරු ලෙස පත් කරන ලද විනිසුරු නලින් පෙරේරා ඩෙප්ගේ තීරණයට එකඟ විය. බුවනෙක අලුවිහාරේ, ප්‍රියන්ත ජයවර්ධන සහ විජිත් කේ මලල්ගොඩ යන විනිසුරුවරුන් විනිසුරු මඩුල්ලේ සෙසු සාමාජිකයින් විය.

දුමින්ද සිල්වාගේ නියමය පරිදි භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ඇතුළු තිදෙනෙකු වෙඩි තබා ඝාතනය කළ බවත්, 2000 දී දෙමළ ඊළාම් විමුක්ති කොටි (එල්ටීටීඊ) විසින් අලිමංකඩ හමුදා කඳවුර ආක්‍රමණය කළ අවස්ථාවේදී භාවිත කළ අවිය සොරාගෙන තිබූ බවත් අධිකරණය තීන්දු කළේය. එවකට ආරක්ෂක අමාත්‍යාංශයේ අධීක්ෂණ මන්ත්‍රීවරයකු වූ සිල්වාට මෙම අවිය ලැබුණේ කෙසේදැයි සඳහන් කර නැත.

ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍රගේ නිල වශයෙන් පවරා ඇති ආරක්ෂකයා විසින් තමාගේ හිසට වෙඩි තබා ඇති බවත්, ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර සහ අනෙකුත් පුද්ගලයන් තිදෙනාගේ ඝාතන ඇතුළු පසුකාලීන සිදුවීම්වලට ඔහු වගකිව යුතු නැති බවත් සිල්වාගේ ස්ථාවරය ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණය ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කළේය. කෙසේ වෙතත්, අධිකරණය මෙම ස්ථාවරය ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කළේ සිල්වා නීතිවිරෝධී රැස්වීමට නායකත්වය දුන් බවත් ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය අවුලුවාලීමත් සමඟ ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය ඇති කළ බවත් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍රගේ පුද්ගලික ආරක්‍ෂක නිලධාරියා පුද්ගලික ආරක්‍ෂාවට ඇති අයිතිය යොදාගෙන වෙඩි තබා සිල්වාට තුවාල කළේය.

“ඔහුගේ හිසට වෙඩි වැදුණු මිනිත්තුව දක්වාම 11 වැනි විත්තිකරු (දුමින්ද සිල්වා) නීති විරෝධී රැස්වීම මෙහෙයවීය. මෙයින් අදහස් කරන්නේ පළමු වෙඩි තැබීම සහ ප්‍රතිප්‍රහාරය අතර කාලයෙහි මිලි තත්පරයක වෙනසක් පමණක් විය හැකි බවයි.

“ගිනි අවි භාවිතයෙන් මරණයට පත් කිරීම ඔවුන්ගේ සාපරාධී ව්‍යාපාරයේ බොහෝ දුරට අපේක්ෂා කළ හැකි ප්‍රතිවිපාකයක් විය. තත්පර 60ක් හෝ ඊට වැඩි කාලයක් පැවති ගනුදෙනුව ආරම්භ වන විට 11 වැනි විත්තිකරු (දුමින්ද සිල්වා) එම සභාවේ සාමාජිකයකු වූ බවද සත්‍යයකි.
"ඒ අතරම, ඊට පෙර කිසිම අවස්ථාවක, 11 වැනි විත්තිකරු (දුමින්ද සිල්වා) රැස්වීමේ වස්තුව සමඟ සම්බන්ධ නොවී සිටීමේ ප්‍රවණතාවක් පෙන්නුම් කළ බවට කිසිදු සාක්ෂියක් නොමැත," තීන්දුවේ සඳහන් විය.

කෙසේ වෙතත්, ගිනි අවියක් භාවිත නොකළ සහ වෙඩි හුවමාරුවට එරෙහිව කටයුතු කළ දුමින්ද සිල්වාගේ නිල ආරක්ෂකයෙකු වන කොස්තාපල් විතානලාගේ අනුර තුෂාර ද මෙල් නිදොස් කොට නිදහස් කිරීමට අධිකරණය නියෝග කළා. ද මෙල් නීති විරෝධී රැස්වීමේ පොදු අරමුණ බෙදාගත් බව තහවුරු කිරීමට පැමිණිල්ල අපොහොසත් වී ඇති බවත් එබැවින් ඔහු නිදොස් කොට නිදහස් කළ යුතු බවත් ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණය ප්‍රකාශ කළේය.

දුමින්ද සිල්වාගේ කල්ලිය භාවිතා කර ඇත්තේ අලිමංකඩ කඳවුරෙන් සොරකම් කරන ලද T56 ගිනි අවියක් පමණක් වන අතර අපරාධය සිදු වූ ස්ථානයේ තිබී සොයාගත් පාවිච්චි කරන ලද කාට්රිජ් 27 ක් වෙඩි තබා ඇත. දුමින්ද සිල්වා එක් ඡන්ද මධ්‍යස්ථානයක සිට අනෙක් ඡන්ද මධ්‍යස්ථානයට ක්‍රමානුකූලව ගොස් ඡන්දදායකයින් බිය ගන්වමින් විපක්ෂයට ඡන්දය දුන් බවට සැක කරන ඡන්දදායකයින්ට පහර දී ඇති බව අධිකරණය විසින් සොයා ගන්නා ලදී. කාන්තාවන් බිය ගැන්වීමට ලක් වූ අතර ප්‍රදේශයේ මිනිසුන්ට එරෙහිව ප්‍රචණ්ඩ ක්‍රියා වැලක් විය.

භාරත ලක්ෂ්මන් ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර ඝාතනය සම්බන්ධයෙන් දුමින්ද සිල්වා මහතාට එරෙහිව නඩු පවරා වරදකරු වීමේදී සිදුවූයේ මෙයයි. දැන් දුමින්ද ජනාධිපති රාජපක්ෂගේ අනුකම්පාව නිසා නිදහස් වෙලා. පැහැදිලි කිරීමක් කර නැත. ජනාධිපතිවරයා විසින් ජූනි 25 වැනි දින රූපවාහිනිය ඔස්සේ ජාතිය අමතා කළ විනාඩි 70ක දේශනයේ ජනපති සමාව ගැන සඳහනක් නොවීය.
මෙම තත්ත්වය යටතේ ශ්‍රී ලංකා BASL සංගමයේ සභාපතිවරයාට ලිඛිතව ලිඛිතව දන්වා ඇත්තේ BASL වෙත සහ සාමාන්‍ය ජනතාවගෙන් මෙසේ ඉල්ලා සිටින ලෙසයි.
(අ) ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාවේ 34 (1) ව්‍යවස්ථාව යටතේ සමාව ලබා දීම සඳහා දුමින්ද සිල්වා තෝරා ගනු ලැබූ පදනම කවරේද;
(ආ) එම සමාව ලබා දීමේදී සැලකිල්ලට ගත් තත්ත්වයන් කවරේද;
(ඇ) දුමින්ද සිල්වාගේ නඩුව දැනට දඬුවම් විඳිමින් සිටින අනෙකුත් අයගෙන් කැපී පෙනෙන්නේ මන්දැයි යන්නට හේතු කවරේද;
(ඈ) දුමින්ද සිල්වාට සමාව ලබා දීමට පෙර 34 (1) වගන්තියේ විධිවිධානයට අනුව අතිගරු ජනාධිපතිතුමා විසින් නඩු විභාගයේ විනිසුරුවරුන්ගෙන් වාර්තාවක් ඉල්ලා සිටියේද යන්න සහ එසේ නම් එම වාර්තාවේ අන්තර්ගතය;
(ඉ) දුමින්ද සිල්වාට සමාව ලබාදීමට පෙර නීතිපතිවරයාගේ උපදෙස් ඉල්ලා සිටියේද යන්න සහ එසේ නම් එම උපදෙස්වල අන්තර්ගතය කවරේද;
(ඊ) දුමින්ද සිල්වාට සමාව ලබාදීමට පෙර අධිකරණ අමාත්‍යවරයාගේ නිර්දේශය ලබා ගත්තේද සහ එසේ නම් අධිකරණ අමාත්‍යවරයා එවැනි නිර්දේශයක් කළේද;

"ඉහත සඳහන් කර ඇති කරුණු එකක් හෝ කිහිපයක් වත්මන් නඩුවේදී සෑහීමකට පත් නොවන්නේ නම්, දුමින්ද සිල්වාට ලබා දී ඇති සමාව අසාධාරණ හා අත්තනෝමතික වන අතර එය නීතියේ ආධිපත්‍යය ඛාදනය වීමට හේතු වන බව ශ්‍රී ලංකා නීතීඥ සංගමය පවසයි. යුක්තිය පසිඳලීම සම්බන්ධයෙන් මහජන විශ්වාසය නැතිවීම,” එය පෙන්වා දුන්නේය.
මෙම ලිපිය 2021 ජුනි 30 දිනැති “ඩේලි එෆ්ටී” තීරුවේ “දේශපාලන ස්පන්දනය” තීරුවේ දිස්වේ. එයට මෙතැනින් ප්‍රවේශ විය හැක:

ta.title: துமிந்த சில்வாவின் சட்ட நடவடிக்கை, நீதித்துறை தண்டனை மற்றும் ஜனாதிபதியின் "விமோசனம்".
body_lines: (குற்றவாளி துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ஷ வழங்கிய ஜனாதிபதி மன்னிப்பைத் தொடர்ந்து ஜூலை 2021 இல் இந்தக் கட்டுரை எழுதப்பட்டது/ ஜனாதிபதியின் பொது மன்னிப்பின் செயல்பாட்டை இடைநிறுத்தி உச்ச நீதிமன்றம் வழங்கிய வரலாற்று இடைக்கால உத்தரவைக் கருத்தில் கொண்டு இப்போது மீண்டும் இடுகையிடப்படுகிறது)

துமிந்த சில்வா என அழைக்கப்படும் அருமதுர லோரன்ஸ் ரொமிலோ துமிந்த சில்வா பொசன் போயா தினத்தில் ஜனாதிபதியின் மன்னிப்பைப் பெற்ற அதிர்ஷ்டசாலி. மரண தண்டனை விதிக்கப்பட்ட குற்றவாளி பொசன் போயா பொது மன்னிப்பின் விளைவாக விடுதலை செய்யப்பட்டார்.
துமிந்த சில்வாவின் சட்ட வழக்கு, நீதித்துறை தண்டனை மற்றும் ஜனாதிபதியின் "விமோசனம்" - டி.பி.எஸ்.ஜெயராஜ்.

முன்னாள் பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினரும் முன்னாள் மாகாண சபை உறுப்பினருமான துமிந்த சில்வா, 2011 ஆம் ஆண்டு ஒக்டோபர் மாதம் உள்ளூராட்சி சபைத் தேர்தலின் போது துப்பாக்கிச் சூட்டுச் சம்பவத்தில் ஈடுபட்டார், அங்கு முன்னாள் பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர் பாரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திர மற்றும் அவரது மெய்ப்பாதுகாவலர்கள் மூவர் கொல்லப்பட்டனர். இலங்கை உயர் நீதிமன்றம் துமிந்த சில்வா மற்றும் அவரது கூட்டாளிகள் நால்வரை கொலைக் குற்றவாளிகளாக அறிவித்து, அவர்களுக்கு 2016 செப்டம்பரில் மரண தண்டனை விதித்தது. அதைத் தொடர்ந்து தண்டனை மேல்முறையீடு செய்யப்பட்டது, ஆனால் உச்ச நீதிமன்றத்தின் ஐந்து நீதிபதிகள் கொண்ட அமர்வு அக்டோபரில் சில்வா மீதான கொலைக் குற்றத்தை உறுதி செய்தது. 2018. 2016 இன் உயர் நீதிமன்றத் தண்டனை 2018 இல் உச்ச நீதிமன்றத்தால் உறுதி செய்யப்பட்ட போதிலும், துமிந்த சில்வா 2021 இல் பொசன் போயா அன்று சுதந்திர மனிதராக இருந்தார்.

ஜனாதிபதி நந்தசேன கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ஷ, அரசியலமைப்பின் 34(1) சரத்தை பிரயோகித்து, ஜூன் 24 பொசன் போயா தினத்தன்று 93 தண்டனை கைதிகளுக்கு ஜனாதிபதி மன்னிப்பு வழங்கினார். இவர்களில் 16 தமிழ் அரசியல் கைதிகள் கொடூரமான பயங்கரவாதத் தடைச் சட்டத்தின் (PTA) கீழ் குற்றம் சாட்டப்பட்டு தண்டனை பெற்றவர்கள். மற்ற 77 கைதிகளில், 76 பேர் ஒப்பீட்டளவில் சிறிய குற்றங்களுக்காக தண்டனை அனுபவித்தவர்கள். இந்த மன்னிப்புகள் மற்றும் வெளியீடுகள் (ஒன்றைத் தவிர) எந்த சர்ச்சையையும் உருவாக்கவில்லை. உண்மையில் தமிழ் கைதிகளின் விடுதலை ஜனாதிபதிக்கு சில பூங்கொத்துகளை கூட சம்பாதித்தது.

எனினும் கொலைக் குற்றவாளி துமிந்த சில்வாவை மன்னித்து விடுதலை செய்தது பெரும் சர்ச்சையை ஏற்படுத்தியது. முன்னாள் நீதியமைச்சர் தலதா அத்துகோரள முதல் அமெரிக்கத் தூதுவர் அலைனா வரை பல்வேறு தரப்பிலிருந்தும் செங்கல்பட்டு பலர் இருந்தனர். பி. டெப்லிட்ஸ். இலங்கை சட்டத்தரணிகள் சங்கம் (BASL) ஒரு விரிவான அறிக்கையில் குற்றவாளியும் முன்னாள் பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினருமான துமிந்த சில்வாவிற்கு ஜனாதிபதியின் மன்னிப்பைக் கண்டித்ததுடன், அது "நியாயமற்றது மற்றும் தன்னிச்சையானது" என்று கூறியது.

இவ்வாறானதொரு பின்னணியில் தான் இந்த பத்தி பரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திர கொலை மற்றும் துமிந்த சில்வாவின் தண்டனை குறித்து கவனம் செலுத்துகிறது. பிரேமச்சந்திரவின் கொலைக்கு வழிவகுத்த நிகழ்வுகளின் வரிசையும் அதன் பின்னரான நீதித்துறை நடவடிக்கைகளும் முந்தைய கட்டுரைகளில் என்னால் விரிவாக எழுதப்பட்டுள்ளன. இந்த வாரம் கடந்த காலத்தை மீண்டும் பார்க்கும்போது இந்த எழுத்துக்களில் சிலவற்றை நான் நம்புவேன்.

சமகால இலங்கை வரலாற்றில் பரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திர சுட்டுக்கொல்லப்பட்டதும் துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு தண்டனை விதிக்கப்பட்டதும் ஒரு சர்ச்சைக்குரிய அத்தியாயமாகும். 2011 ஆம் ஆண்டு ஒக்டோபர் 8 ஆம் திகதி கொழும்பு நகரிலிருந்து ஆறு மைல்களுக்கு அப்பால் உள்ள முல்லேரியா நகருக்கு அண்மித்த வல்பொல சந்திக்கு அருகில் இடம்பெற்ற துப்பாக்கிச் சூட்டில் “லக்கி ஐயா” என அழைக்கப்படும் பரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திரவும் அவரது மெய்ப்பாதுகாவலர்கள் மூவரும் கொல்லப்பட்டனர். மற்றொரு மெய்க்காப்பாளர் பலத்த காயம் அடைந்தார்.

அன்றைய தினம் பல உள்ளாட்சி அமைப்புகளுக்கான தேர்தல் நடைபெற்றது. இவற்றில் கொட்டிகாவத்தை - முல்லேரியா பிரதேச சபையும் இருந்தது. சம்பவம் நடந்த அன்று பரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திர பரபரப்பான பிரச்சாரத்தில் ஈடுபட்டிருந்தார்.

இந்த மோதல் இரு அரசியல் கட்சிகளுக்கு இடையே இல்லை. இது சக ஐக்கிய மக்கள் சுதந்திரக் கூட்டமைப்பு (UPFA) செயற்பாட்டாளர்களின் இரு குழுக்களுக்கிடையில் இருந்தது. கொழும்பு முன்னாள் பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர் பாரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திர தலைமையில் ஒரு குழு அமைக்கப்பட்டது. மற்றையது அப்போதைய கொழும்பு மாவட்ட பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர் துமிந்த சில்வா தலைமையில். கொட்டிகாவத்தை - முல்லேரியா பிரதேச சபைத் தேர்தல் பிரேமச்சந்திர மற்றும் சில்வாவிற்கு இடையில் சுதந்திர முன்னணிக்குள் விருப்பு வாக்குகளுக்கான "பினாமி சண்டை" ஆகும். பரத தனது சீடரும் தற்போதைய தலைவருமான பிரசன்ன சோலங்கராச்சிக்கு ஆதரவளித்தார். துமிந்த சில்வா அவருக்கு எதிராக செயற்பட்டதுடன் இன்னொருவருக்கு முட்டுக்கொடுத்தார்.

பிரேமச்சந்திர இறக்கும் போது ஜனாதிபதி மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷவின் தொழிற்சங்க ஆலோசகராக கடமையாற்றினார். முன்னாள் கொழும்பு மாவட்ட பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர் 2004 மற்றும் 2010 தேர்தல்களில் தோல்வியடைந்தார். அவருக்கு பதிலாக கொலன்னாவ தேர்தல் அமைப்பாளராக துமிந்த சில்வா நியமிக்கப்பட்டார். இதனால் வெறுப்படைந்த பிரேமச்சந்திர, அவருக்கும் துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கும் இடையில் அரசியல் களப்போர் ஏற்பட்டது. உள்ளாட்சி மன்றக் கருத்துக் கணிப்பு ஒரு கௌரவப் போராக மாறியது.

பிரச்சாரம் மோசமானது மற்றும் வன்முறையால் சிதைந்தது. மூன்று தசாப்தகால அரசியல் அனுபவமுள்ள பரத லக்ஷ்மன் போதைப்பொருள் மற்றும் போதைப்பொருள் வியாபாரிகளுக்கு எதிரான அறவழிப்போராட்டமாக மாற்றினார். கொலன்னாவையில் "குடுகராய" அச்சுறுத்தலுக்கு எதிரான மக்கள் விரோதப் போக்கு வாக்காளர்களின் இதயங்களிலும் மனங்களிலும் ஒரு பதிலளிப்பதைத் தாக்கியது.

முடிவுகள் அறிவிக்கப்பட்டபோது அது பரதனுக்கு கிடைத்த மாபெரும் அரசியல் வெற்றி. அவரது "வேட்பாளருக்கு" 42,322 விருப்பத்தேர்வுகள் கிடைத்துள்ளது. அவரது போட்டியாளரின் வேட்பாளர் 5,811 விருப்பத்தேர்வுகளை மட்டுமே பெற்றிருந்தார். போதைப்பொருள் மாஃபியாவுக்கு எதிரான பாரத லக்ஷ்மனின் போரை வாக்காளர்கள் பெருமளவில் அங்கீகரித்துள்ளனர். இந்த நடவடிக்கையில் பிரேமச்சந்திர தான் இன்னும் ஒரு அரசியல் சக்தியாக அந்தப் பிரதேசத்தில் இருப்பதை நிரூபித்திருந்தார். துரதிர்ஷ்டவசமாக பரத லக்ஷ்மணன் தனது வெற்றியை அனுபவிக்க உயிருடன் இல்லை. விதியின் அசையும் விரல் ஏற்கனவே அவனது கல்வெட்டை எழுதியிருந்தது.

ஊடக அறிக்கையின்படி, உள்ளாட்சித் தேர்தல் நாளில் பரதன் ஒரு பகுதியிலும், சோலங்கராச்சி மற்றொரு பகுதியிலும் பிரச்சாரத்தில் ஈடுபட்டுள்ளனர். துமிந்த சில்வா மற்றும் அவரது குழுவினர் மற்றுமொரு பிரதேசத்தில் பிரச்சாரம் செய்து கொண்டிருந்த போது “தமிழ்நாடுவத்தை” என்ற பகுதியில் மதுபானம் அருந்தி உற்சாகமாக இருந்ததாக கூறப்படுகிறது. ஒரு கட்டத்தில் துமிந்தவும் அவரது கூட்டாளிகளும் சோலங்கராச்சியின் குடியிருப்புக்கு அருகாமையில் உள்ள வாக்குச் சாவடிக்கு சென்றனர். துமிந்த சோலங்கராச்சியின் மனைவி மதுவிடம் யாருக்கு வாக்களித்தீர்கள் என்று கேட்டார். அப்போது வாய் தகராறு ஏற்பட்டது. மதுவின் மாமியார்/சோலங்கஆராச்சியின் தாயார் கண்டித்தபோது, ​​துமிந்த சில்வா அவளையும் ஆபாசமான வார்த்தைகளால் திட்டியுள்ளார். இரு பெண்களும் முரட்டுத்தனமாக நடத்தப்பட்டதாக சிலர் குற்றம் சாட்டுகின்றனர். துமிந்தவும் அந்த இடத்தில் சோலங்கராச்சியின் ஆதரவாளர்களை தொடர்ந்து மிரட்டி அச்சுறுத்தி வந்தார்.

பரத லட்சுமணனுக்கு என்ன நடக்கிறது என்று தெரிவிக்கப்பட்டது. அவர் சம்பவ இடத்திற்கு அருகில் இருந்ததால், பரதன் தனது தோழர்களுடன் அப்பகுதிக்கு விரைந்தார். பரதன் வருவதாகக் கேள்விப்பட்ட துமிந்த அந்த இடத்தை விட்டு வெளியேறினார். வல்பொல சந்திக்கு அருகில் உள்ள வீதியில் இரு வாகனத் தொடரணிகளும் சந்தித்தன. பரத மற்றும் துமிந்த இருவரும் தங்கள் வாகனங்களில் இருந்து இறங்கி ஒருவரை ஒருவர் நோக்கி நகர்ந்தனர்.

கோழைத்தனமாக பெண்களை மிரட்டி பயமுறுத்துவது ஏன் என துமிந்தவிடம் பரத கேட்டான். துமிந்த திடீரென பரதனை தாக்கியதுடன் உதைத்துள்ளார். ஆச்சரியத்தில் பரதன் கீழே விழுந்தான். இந்த நிலையில் துமிந்த தனது ஆட்களை துப்பாக்கிச் சூடு நடத்த உத்தரவிட்டார். நிலைமையின் தீவிரத்தை உணர்ந்து, பரதனின் ஆயுதமேந்திய "அதிகாரப்பூர்வ" மெய்க்காப்பாளரும் துப்பாக்கிச் சூடு நடத்தினார். அவரது அதிகாரப்பூர்வமற்ற மெய்க்காப்பாளர்களாக பணியாற்றிய பரதவின் தோழர்கள் நிராயுதபாணிகளாக இருந்தனர். பரதனும் அவனது மூன்று தோழர்களும் சுட்டுக் கொல்லப்பட்டனர். பரதன் தரையில் படுத்திருந்த போது துப்பாக்கியால் சுட்டார். அவரது உத்தியோகபூர்வ மெய்ப்பாதுகாவலரும் காயமடைந்துள்ளார்.

குற்றவாளிகள் கைது செய்யப்படுவதற்குப் பதிலாக, ஒரு பாரிய மூடிமறைப்புப் பயிற்சியாகத் தோன்றியதன் மூலம் நாடு அரசமைக்கப்பட்டது. துப்பாக்கிச் சூட்டில் துமிந்த சில்வா படுகாயமடைந்துள்ளதாக தெரிவிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது. அவர் மருத்துவமனையில் அனுமதிக்கப்பட்டார். அப்போதைய ஜனாதிபதி மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷவும் அவரது பாதுகாப்புச் செயலாளர் கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ஷவும் துமிந்தவை வைத்தியசாலைக்கு சென்று பார்வையிட்டனர். சிறப்பு பாதுகாப்பு அளிக்கப்பட்டது. வெளியாட்கள் துமிந்தவிற்கு அருகில் செல்ல அனுமதிக்கப்படவில்லை. இறுதியாக அவர் பலத்த பாதுகாப்புக்கு மத்தியில் விமானம் மூலம் சிங்கப்பூருக்கு அழைத்துச் செல்லப்பட்டார். அவருக்கு பல காயங்கள் ஏற்பட்டுள்ளதாகவும், கூடுதல் மருத்துவ சிகிச்சை தேவைப்படுவதாகவும் கூறப்பட்டது. பின்னர் இலங்கைக்கு திரும்பிய துமிந்த, தான் பகுதியளவு ஞாபக மறதியால் பாதிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளதாகவும், குறித்த நாளில் என்ன நடந்தது என்பது நினைவில் இல்லை எனவும் அதிகாரிகளிடம் தெரிவித்தார்.
துமிந்த சில்வா ஜனாதிபதியின் மன்னிப்பின் விளைவாக ஜூன் 24 அன்று விடுவிக்கப்பட்டார் - டெய்லி எப்டி மூலம் புகைப்படம்

பயனுள்ள சட்ட நடவடிக்கைகள் தொடங்கப்படுவதில் மிதமிஞ்சிய தாமதம் ஏற்பட்டது. சட்டத்தின் தாமதங்களுக்கு சொற்பொழிவாகக் கூறப்பட்ட நீண்ட கால ஒத்திவைப்பு இருந்தது. எனினும், துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கும் மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷ ஆட்சியில் இருந்த பலம் வாய்ந்த நபர்களுக்கும் இடையிலான நெருங்கிய உறவே முட்டுக்கட்டையாக இருந்ததாக பெரும்பாலான மக்கள் சந்தேகிக்கின்றனர். இந்த விவகாரம் பல ஆண்டுகளாக இழுத்துச் செல்லப்பட்டது, மேலும் "தாமதமான நீதி நீதி மறுக்கப்பட்டதாகும்" என்ற பழமொழி இந்த நிகழ்விலும் உண்மையாக நிரூபிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது என்று பரவலாக நம்பப்பட்டது.

ஆளும் ராஜபக்ச ஸ்தாபனத்திற்குள் பணிபுரியும் போது நீதியைப் பாதுகாப்பதற்கான புரிந்துகொள்ளக்கூடிய ஆனால் அப்பாவித்தனமான முயற்சியில், பிரேமச்சந்திரவின் விதவை மற்றும் மகள் ஜனாதிபதி மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷவுடன் நெருக்கமாக தொடர்பு கொண்டிருந்தனர். மறைந்த கணவரைப் போலவே சுமனா பிரேமச்சந்திரவும் ஜனாதிபதி ராஜபக்சவின் ஆலோசகராக நியமிக்கப்பட்டார்.

மகள் ஹிருணிகா பிரேமச்சந்திர, ஆளும் ராஜபக்ச ஆட்சியில் தன்னை அடையாளப்படுத்திக் கொண்டார். 2014 இல் அவர் மேல் மாகாண சபைக்கான தேர்தலில் UPFA சார்பில் போட்டியிட்டார். அவர் கொழும்பு மாவட்டத்தில் இருந்து வெற்றி பெற்று விருப்பு வாக்குகள் பட்டியலில் முதலிடம் பிடித்தார். இத்தனை முயற்சிகள் இருந்தபோதிலும், பரதத்திற்கான நீதிக்கான தேடல் எப்போதும் போல் மழுப்பலாகத் தோன்றியது.

2014 நவம்பரில் மைத்திரிபால சிறிசேன அரசாங்கத்தை முறித்துக் கொண்டபோது ஹிருணிகாவுக்கு வாய்ப்பு கிடைத்தது. பொலன்னறுவையில் இருந்து நட்சத்திரத்திற்கு தனது வேகனை ஏற்றி அந்த தருணத்தை அவர் கைப்பற்றினார். 2015 இல் ஹிருணிகா ஐக்கிய தேசியக் கட்சியின் (UNP) சீட்டில் கொழும்பு மாவட்டத்தில் போட்டியிட்டு வெற்றி பெற்றார். எனினும் ஜனாதிபதி மைத்திரிபால சிறிசேனவினால் துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு வேட்புமனு நிராகரிக்கப்பட்டது.

2015 ஆம் ஆண்டு ஜனாதிபதி மைத்திரிபால சிறிசேன மற்றும் பிரதமர் ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்கவின் கீழ் புதிய ஆட்சியமைப்பின் வருகையானது நீதித்துறைப் பாதைகளில் புதிய காற்று வீசுவதைக் கண்டது. பரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திர, தர்ஷன ஜயதிலக, மொஹமட் அஸ்மி மற்றும் மணிவேல் குமாரசுவாமி ஆகியோரின் கொலைகள் மற்றும் இராஜபுரகே காமினி மீது துப்பாக்கிச் சூட்டுக் காயங்களை ஏற்படுத்தியமைக்காக 2015 மார்ச் மாதம் சட்டமா அதிபர் நீதிமன்றில் குற்றப்பத்திரிகை தாக்கல் செய்தார்.
துமிந்த சில்வா உள்ளிட்ட 13 சந்தேகநபர்களுக்கு எதிராக 17 குற்றச்சாட்டுக்கள் சுமத்தப்பட்டிருந்தன.

பிரதம நீதியரசர் கனகசபாபதி ஸ்ரீபவன் 2015 ஆம் ஆண்டு மே மாதம் ட்ரயல்-அட்-பார் நடவடிக்கைகளுக்காக மூன்று பேர் கொண்ட பெஞ்சை நியமித்தார். உயர் நீதிமன்ற நீதிபதிகளான ஷிரான் குணரத்ன, பத்மினி ரணவக்க மற்றும் எம்.சி.பி.எஸ். மோரேஸ். தலைவராக ஷிரான் குணரத்ன இருந்தார்.

2015 ஆம் ஆண்டு மே 22 ஆம் திகதி வழக்கு விசாரணைகள் ஆரம்பமாகியது. இந்த வழக்கு 2015 செப்டெம்பர் 12 ஆம் திகதி முதல் தொடர்ச்சியாக விசாரணைக்கு வந்தது. சந்தன ஜகத் குமார, லங்கா ரசாஞ்சன, மாலக சமீர, விதானகமகே அமில, சுரங்க பிரேமலால், சமன் குமார ஆகிய 13 பேரின் பெயர்கள் , சமன் அபேவிக்ரம, ரோஹன மாரசிங்க, துமிந்த சில்வா, அனுர துஷார டி மெல், தெமட்டகொட சமிந்த எனப்படும் சமிந்த ரவி ஜயநாத், திஸாநாயக்க முதியன்சேலாகே சரத் பண்டார மற்றும் ஜனக பண்டார கலகொட. கடைசியாக பெயரிடப்பட்டவர் தலைமறைவாக இருந்ததால் அவர் ஆஜராகாமல் விசாரணைக்கு உட்படுத்தப்பட்டார். குற்றம் சாட்டப்பட்டவர்கள் மீது குற்றவியல் சட்டத்தின் 296, 140, 146, 147, 486 மற்றும் 300 மற்றும் துப்பாக்கிச் சட்டத்தின் சில பிரிவுகளின் கீழ் குற்றம் சாட்டப்பட்டது.

17 குற்றச்சாட்டுகளில் நான்கு நபர்களை கொலை செய்தல் மற்றும் சதி செய்தல், கொலை முயற்சி, சட்டவிரோத துப்பாக்கி வைத்திருந்தல், துப்பாக்கியால் சுட்டு காயப்படுத்துதல், சட்டவிரோத கூட்டம் மற்றும் குற்றவியல் மிரட்டல் ஆகியவை அக்டோபர் 8, 2011 அன்று அல்லது அதற்கு அருகில் உள்ளவை. வழக்குரைஞர் சார்பில் சொலிசிட்டர் ஜெனரல் துசித் முதலிகே வாதிட்டார். நீதிமன்றங்களில் 42 சாட்சிகள் சாட்சியமளித்தனர் மற்றும் அரசாங்க பகுப்பாய்வாளர் அறிக்கைகள் மற்றும் JMO அறிக்கைகள் உட்பட 126 ஆவணங்கள் சமர்ப்பிக்கப்பட்டன.

ட்ரையல்-அட்-பார் நடவடிக்கைகள் 14 ஜூலை 2016 அன்று முடிவடைந்தது. உயர்மட்ட விசாரணையின் தீர்ப்பு செப்டம்பர் 8, 2016 அன்று வழங்கப்பட்டது. மூன்று நீதிபதிகளில் இருவர் குற்றம் சாட்டப்பட்டவர்களில் ஐவரைக் குற்றவாளிகள் எனக் கண்டறிந்து மேலும் 8 பேரை விடுவித்து ஒரு பிரிக்கப்பட்ட தீர்ப்பாகும்.

எவ்வாறாயினும், குற்றம் சாட்டப்பட்ட 13 பேரையும் குற்றவாளிகள் இல்லை என்று ஒரு நீதிபதி அறிவித்தார். ட்ரயல்-அட்-பார்க்கு தலைமை தாங்கிய உயர் நீதிமன்ற நீதிபதி ஷிரான் குணரத்ன, கொலை வழக்கு விசாரணையில் குற்றம் சாட்டப்பட்ட 13 பேரையும் விடுவித்தார். சந்தேகத்தின் பலன் குற்றம் சாட்டப்பட்டவர்களுக்கே செல்ல வேண்டும் என்றார். சக உயர் நீதிமன்ற நீதிபதிகளான பத்மினி ரணவக்க மற்றும் எம்.சி.பி.எஸ். மோரேஸ் குணரத்னவிடம் இருந்து வேறுபட்டார். இதன் விளைவாக இரண்டுக்கு ஒரு பெரும்பான்மை தீர்ப்பு கிடைத்தது.

உயர் நீதிமன்ற நீதிபதி பத்மினி என். ரணவக்க, நிரம்பிய நீதிமன்ற வளாகத்தில், நீதிபதி மொரேஸ் இணக்கத்துடன் இரண்டு பெரும்பான்மை தீர்ப்பை வழங்கினார். 11வது குற்றவாளியான துமிந்த சில்வாவின் ஆத்திரமூட்டும் விதத்தில் இந்த முழு சம்பவத்திற்கு வழிவகுத்தது என்பதை வழக்கு விசாரணையின் போது அரசு தரப்பு நிரூபித்ததாக நீதிபதி பத்மினி ரணவக்க பெரும்பான்மை தீர்ப்பை வழங்கினார்.

துமிந்த சில்வா கும்பலின் கட்டுக்கடங்காத நடத்தைக்கு தலைமைத்துவத்தை வழங்கியிருந்தார் என்பதை சமர்பித்த சாட்சியங்கள் நிரூபித்துள்ளதாகவும் எனவே முழு சம்பவத்திற்கும் அவரே பொறுப்பேற்க வேண்டும் எனவும் நீதிபதி மேலும் குறிப்பிட்டார். சம்பவத்தின் போது துமிந்த சில்வா மது போதையில் இருந்ததாக ஜே.எம்.ஓ அறிக்கைகள் வெளிப்படுத்தியதாகவும், ஆனால் இறந்த பாரத பிரேமச்சந்திர அப்படி இருக்கவில்லை என்றும் அவர் கூறினார். சில்வா முதலில் பிரேமச்சந்திர தரப்பினரால் சுடப்பட்டதாக விசாரணையின் போது பிரதிவாதிகள் முன்வைத்த வாதத்தையும் தீர்ப்பில் நிராகரித்துள்ளது.

பின்னர் குற்றம் சாட்டப்பட்ட 8 பேரை நீதிமன்றம் விடுவித்து விடுதலை செய்தது. அவர்கள் – சந்தன ஜகத் குமார, லங்கா ரசாஞ்சன, மாலக சமீர, விதானகமகே அமில, சுரங்க பிரேமலால், சமன் குமார, சமன் அபேவிக்ரம மற்றும் ரோஹன மாரசிங்க. ஐந்து பேருக்கு மரண தண்டனை விதிக்கப்பட்டது. அவர்கள் – துமிந்த சில்வா, அனுர துஷார டி மெல், தெமட்டகொட சமிந்த எனப்படும் சமிந்த ரவி ஜயநாத், திஸாநாயக்க முதியன்சேலாகே சரத் பண்டார மற்றும் ஜனக பண்டார கலகொட. 10வது குற்றவாளியான ஜனக பண்டார கலகொட ஆஜராகாத நிலையில் விசாரணைக்கு உட்படுத்தப்பட்டு குற்றவாளியாக அறிவிக்கப்பட்டார். மரணதண்டனை தீர்ப்புக்கு கூடுதலாக, குற்றவாளிகள் ஐந்து பேருக்கும் ரூ. தலா 40,000.

ட்ரயல்-அட்-பார் உயர்நீதிமன்ற தீர்ப்பை எதிர்த்து உச்ச நீதிமன்றத்தில் மேல்முறையீடு செய்யப்பட்டது. அப்போதைய பிரதம நீதியரசர் பிரியசாத் டெப் தலைமையிலான 5 நீதிபதிகள் கொண்ட உச்ச நீதிமன்ற அமர்வு இந்த தண்டனையை ஒருமனதாக உறுதி செய்தது. தலைமை நீதிபதி டெப் 11 அக்டோபர் 2018 அன்று 51 பக்க தீர்ப்பை வெளியிட்டார். அக்டோபர் 13 அன்று ‘எகனாமி நெக்ஸ்ட்’ இணையதளத்தில் வெளியிடப்பட்ட சுருக்கமான செய்தி அறிக்கை கீழே கொடுக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது:

பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர் துமிந்த சில்வாவின் மரண தண்டனையை உறுதி செய்த இலங்கையின் உச்ச நீதிமன்றம், ஆனையிறவு இராணுவ முகாமில் இருந்து திருடப்பட்ட தானியங்கி ஆயுதத்தை பயன்படுத்தியமை உள்ளிட்ட குற்றச் செயல்களையும் முன்னிலைப்படுத்தியுள்ளது. பதவி விலகும் பிரதம நீதியரசர் பிரியசாத் டெப் தனது 51 பக்க உத்தரவில், சில்வா மற்றும் அவரது கூட்டாளிகளால் மேற்கொள்ளப்பட்ட தேர்தல் சட்ட மீறல்கள் உட்பட, பரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திர மற்றும் மூன்று பேரின் கொலையில் உச்சக்கட்டத்தை அடைந்தது உட்பட குற்றங்களின் சரத்தை குறிப்பிட்டுள்ளார். அக்டோபர் 2011 உள்ளூராட்சி மன்றத் தேர்தலில் சில்வா மற்றும் அவரது கும்பல் போட்டி ஆதரவாளர்கள் மற்றும் வாக்காளர்களை அடித்து நொறுக்கிய பின்னர், பெண்களை மிரட்டியதை அடுத்து இந்த கொலைகள் நடந்ததாக ஐந்து நீதிபதிகள் கொண்ட பெஞ்ச் அதன் ஏகமனதாக தீர்ப்பளித்தது.

வாக்குப்பதிவு ஆரம்பமானது முதல் அது முடியும் வரை 11வது குற்றவாளியான (துமிந்த சில்வா) வாக்களிப்பு நிலையங்களுக்கு இடையில் ஆயுதங்களுடன் சூறையாடுவதும், அதிகாரிகளின் கடமைகளை மீறுவதும், சம்பந்தப்பட்டவர்களை தாக்குவதும், பலிவாங்குவதுமாக தனது நாளைக் கழித்தார். சோலங்கராச்சி (ஒரு போட்டி வேட்பாளர்).குழு மதிய உணவு சாப்பிடும் போது மட்டுமே அவர்கள் மக்களை அச்சுறுத்துவதைக் காணவில்லை, ”என்று தலைமை நீதிபதி ஓய்வு பெறுவதற்கு முன் தனது இறுதி உத்தரவில் கூறினார்.

வெள்ளிக்கிழமையன்று பிரதம நீதியரசராக நியமிக்கப்பட்ட நீதியரசர் நளின் பெரேரா, டெப்பின் முடிவை ஏற்றுக்கொண்டார். நீதியரசர்களான புவனேக அலுவிஹாரே, பிரியந்த ஜயவர்தன மற்றும் விஜித் கே. மலல்கொட ஆகியோர் பீடத்தில் இருந்தனர்.

துமிந்த சில்வாவின் உத்தரவின் பேரில் பாரத லக்ஷ்மன் மற்றும் மூவர் சுட்டுக் கொல்லப்பட்டதாகவும், 2000 ஆம் ஆண்டு தமிழீழ விடுதலைப் புலிகளால் ஆனையிறவு இராணுவ முகாமை கைப்பற்றிய போது பயன்படுத்தப்பட்ட ஆயுதம் அதிலிருந்து திருடப்பட்டதாகவும் நீதிமன்றம் தீர்ப்பளித்தது. அப்போது பாதுகாப்பு அமைச்சின் கண்காணிப்பு எம்.பி.யாக இருந்த சில்வாவுக்கு இந்த ஆயுதம் எப்படி வந்தது என்பது குறிப்பிடப்படவில்லை.

பிரேமச்சந்திரவின் உத்தியோகபூர்வமாக நியமிக்கப்பட்ட மெய்ப்பாதுகாவலரால் தான் தலையில் சுடப்பட்டதாகவும், பிரேமச்சந்திர மற்றும் ஏனைய மூன்று பேரின் கொலைகள் உட்பட அடுத்தடுத்த நிகழ்வுகளுக்கு அவர் பொறுப்பல்ல என்ற சில்வாவின் நிலைப்பாட்டை உச்ச நீதிமன்றம் நிராகரித்தது. எவ்வாறாயினும், இந்த நிலைப்பாட்டை நீதிமன்றம் நிராகரித்தது, சில்வா சட்டவிரோத கூட்டத்திற்கு தலைமை தாங்கினார் மற்றும் வன்முறையைத் தூண்டினார், முதலில் பிரேமச்சந்திரவின் தனிப்பட்ட பாதுகாப்பு அதிகாரி, தனிப்பட்ட பாதுகாப்பு உரிமையைப் பயன்படுத்தி, துப்பாக்கிச் சூடு நடத்தி சில்வாவை காயப்படுத்தினார்.

“அவர் தலையில் சுடப்பட்ட நிமிடம் வரை, 11வது குற்றவாளியான (துமிந்த சில்வா) சட்டவிரோத கூட்டத்திற்கு தலைமை தாங்கினார். அதாவது முதல் ஷாட்டுக்கும் பதிலடிக்கும் இடையே ஒரு மில்லி விநாடி வித்தியாசம் மட்டுமே இருந்திருக்கும்.

"துப்பாக்கிகளைப் பயன்படுத்தி மரணத்தை ஏற்படுத்துவது அவர்களின் கிரிமினல் நிறுவனத்தின் எதிர்பார்க்கக்கூடிய விளைவாகும். 60 அல்லது அதற்கு மேற்பட்ட வினாடிகள் நீடித்த இந்த பரிவர்த்தனை தொடங்கும் போது 11வது குற்றவாளி (துமிந்த சில்வா) அந்த சபையில் உறுப்பினராக இருந்தார் என்பதும் உண்மை.

"அதே நேரத்தில், அதற்கு முன்னர் எந்த நேரத்திலும், 11 வது குற்றவாளி (துமிந்த சில்வா) சபையின் பொருளுடன் தன்னைத் துண்டித்துக் கொள்ளும் போக்கைக் காட்டினார் என்பதற்கு எந்த ஆதாரமும் இல்லை" என்று தீர்ப்பில் கூறப்பட்டுள்ளது.

எவ்வாறாயினும், ஆயுதம் பயன்படுத்தாத துமிந்த சில்வாவின் உத்தியோகபூர்வ மெய்ப்பாதுகாவலரான கான்ஸ்டபிள் விதானலகே அனுர துஷார டி மெல், துப்பாக்கிச் சண்டையில் ஈடுபடுவதற்கு எதிராக செயற்பட்ட துமிந்த சில்வாவை நீதிமன்றம் விடுவித்தது. சட்டத்திற்குப் புறம்பான கூட்டத்தின் பொதுவான நோக்கத்தை டி மெல் பகிர்ந்து கொண்டார் என்பதை நிறுவ அரசுத் தரப்பு தவறிவிட்டதாகவும், எனவே அவர் விடுவிக்கப்பட வேண்டும் என்றும் உச்ச நீதிமன்றம் கூறியது.

துமிந்த சில்வாவின் கும்பல் ஆனையிறவு முகாமில் இருந்து திருடப்பட்ட T56 ஆயுதம் ஒன்றே பயன்படுத்தியிருந்ததுடன், குற்றம் நடந்த இடத்தில் கிடைத்த 27 தோட்டாக்கள் அதிலிருந்து சுடப்பட்டுள்ளன. துமிந்த சில்வா வாக்காளர்களை அச்சுறுத்தும் வகையில் ஒரு வாக்களிப்பு நிலையத்திலிருந்து மற்றுமொரு வாக்களிப்பு நிலையத்திற்கு திட்டமிட்டுச் சென்றதாகவும், எதிரணிக்கு வாக்களித்ததாக சந்தேகிக்கப்படும் வாக்காளர்களைத் தாக்கியதாகவும் நீதிமன்றம் கண்டறிந்துள்ளது. பெண்களை மிரட்டியதோடு, அப்பகுதியில் மக்களுக்கு எதிராக தொடர் வன்முறையும் நடந்துள்ளது.

பரத லக்ஷ்மன் பிரேமச்சந்திர கொலை வழக்கில் துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு எதிராக வழக்குத் தொடரப்பட்டு, தண்டனை விதிக்கப்பட்டபோது இதுதான் நடந்தது. தற்போது அதிபர் ராஜபக்சேவின் கருணையால் துமிந்த விடுதலை செய்யப்பட்டுள்ளார். எந்த விளக்கமும் கொடுக்கப்படவில்லை. ஜூன் 25 அன்று ஜனாதிபதியின் 70 நிமிட தொலைக்காட்சி உரையில் ஜனாதிபதியின் மன்னிப்பு பற்றிய குறிப்பு எதுவும் இல்லை.
இந்த சூழ்நிலையில், இலங்கை BASL சட்டத்தரணிகள் சங்கம் ஜனாதிபதிக்கு கடிதம் எழுதியுள்ளதுடன், ஜனாதிபதி BASL மற்றும் பொது மக்களுக்கும் கோரிக்கை விடுத்துள்ளது:
(அ) ​​அரசியலமைப்பின் 34 (1) பிரிவின் கீழ் மன்னிப்பு வழங்குவதற்காக துமிந்த சில்வா தேர்ந்தெடுக்கப்பட்டதன் அடிப்படையில்;
(ஆ) அத்தகைய மன்னிப்பை வழங்குவதில் கவனத்தில் கொள்ளப்பட்ட சூழ்நிலைகள்;
(இ) துமிந்த சில்வாவின் வழக்கு தற்போது தண்டனை விதிக்கப்பட்டுள்ள மற்றவர்களை விட ஏன் தனித்து நிற்கிறது என்பதற்கான காரணங்கள்;

(ஈ) துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு மன்னிப்பு வழங்குவதற்கு முன்னர் 34 (1) வது சரத்தின் விதியின்படி அதிமேதகு ஜனாதிபதியால் விசாரணை நீதிபதிகளிடமிருந்து அறிக்கை கோரப்பட்டதா மற்றும் அவ்வாறாயின் அந்த அறிக்கையின் உள்ளடக்கங்கள்;
(இ) துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு மன்னிப்பு வழங்குவதற்கு முன்னர் சட்டமா அதிபரின் ஆலோசனை கோரப்பட்டதா மற்றும் அவ்வாறாயின் அத்தகைய ஆலோசனையின் உள்ளடக்கங்கள் யாவை;
(ஊ) துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு மன்னிப்பு வழங்குவதற்கு முன்னர் நீதி அமைச்சரின் பரிந்துரை பெறப்பட்டதா மற்றும் அவ்வாறாயின் நீதி அமைச்சர் அத்தகைய பரிந்துரையை செய்தாரா;

"மேலே கூறப்பட்ட ஏதேனும் ஒன்று அல்லது அதற்கு மேற்பட்ட கருத்தாய்வுகள் தற்போதைய வழக்கில் திருப்தியடையவில்லை என்றால், துமிந்த சில்வாவுக்கு வழங்கப்பட்ட மன்னிப்பு நியாயமற்றது மற்றும் தன்னிச்சையானது மற்றும் சட்டத்தின் ஆட்சிக்கு சிதைவை ஏற்படுத்தும் என்று இலங்கை சட்டத்தரணிகள் சங்கம் கூறுகிறது. நீதி நிர்வாகத்தின் மீது பொதுமக்களின் நம்பிக்கையை இழந்துள்ளது,” என்று அது சுட்டிக்காட்டியது.
இந்தக் கட்டுரை ஜூன் 30, 2021 தேதியிட்ட “டெய்லி எஃப்டி” பத்திரிக்கையின் “அரசியல் துடிப்பு” நெடுவரிசையில் உள்ளது. இதை இங்கே அணுகலாம்:

en.title: The  Legal Prosecution,  Judicial Conviction  and  Presidential “Absolution” of Duminda Silva.
body_lines: (This  Article was written in July 2021 following the Presidential Pardon given to Convicted Murderer Duminda Silva by President Gotabaya  Rajapaksa/It is being re-posted now in view of the historic interim order issued by the Supreme Court suspending the operation of the  Presidential Pardon)

Arumadura Lawrence Romelo Duminda Silva known as Duminda Silva was the fortunate recipient of a munificent Presidential pardon on Poson Poya day. The convict on death row was released as a result of the Poson Poya pardon.
The  Legal Prosecution,  Judicial Conviction  and  Presidential “Absolution” of Duminda Silva – by D.B.S.JEYARAJ

Duminda Silva, a former Parliamentarian and ex-Provincial Councillor, was involved in a shooting incident in October 2011 during Local Authority Elections, where former MP Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra and three of his bodyguards were killed. The High Court of Sri Lanka convicted Duminda Silva and four of his associates for murder and imposed the death sentence on them in September 2016. Subsequently the sentence was appealed but a five-Judge bench of the Supreme Court upheld the murder conviction of Silva in October 2018. Despite the High Court conviction of 2016 being upheld by the Supreme Court in 2018, Duminda Silva was a free man in 2021 on Poson Poya.

President Nandasena Gotabaya Rajapaksa invoking article 34(1) of the Constitution, granted a Presidential pardon to 93 convicted prisoners on Poson Poya day of 24 June. Of these 16 were Tamil political prisoners charged and convicted under the draconian Prevention of Terrorism Act (PTA). Of the 77 other prisoners, 76 were people who had been serving sentences for relatively minor offences. These pardons and releases (except for one) did not create any controversy. In fact the release of Tamil prisoners even earned the President some bouquets.

Murder convict Duminda Silva’s pardon and release however caused much controversy. There were many brickbats from various quarters ranging from former Justice Minister Thalatha Athukorale to US Ambassador Alaina. B. Teplitz. The Bar Association of Sri Lanka (BASL) in a detailed statement condemned the Presidential pardon for convict and former MP Duminda Silva and stated it was “unreasonable and arbitrary”.

It is against this backdrop therefore that this column focuses on the murder of Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra and the conviction of Duminda Silva. The sequence of events leading to Premachandra’s killing and the judicial proceedings in its aftermath have been written in detail by me in earlier articles. I shall rely on some of these writings as I revisit the past this week.

The shooting of Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra and conviction of Duminda Silva was a controversial episode in contemporary Sri Lankan history. Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra known as “Lucky Aiya” and three of his bodyguards were killed on 8 October 2011 in a shoot-out near the Walpola junction close to Mulleriyawa town, about six miles away from Colombo city. Another bodyguard was seriously injured.

Elections to several Local Authorities were being held on that day. Among these was the Kotikawatte-Mulleriyawa Pradeshiya Sabha. Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra had been engaged in hectic canvassing on that day when the incident occurred.

The clash was not between two rival political parties. It was between two groups of fellow United People’s Freedom Alliance (UPFA) activists. One group was led by former Colombo MP Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra. The other was led by the then Colombo District MP Duminda Silva. The Kotikawatte-Mulleriyawa Pradeshiya Sabha election was a “proxy battle” for preference votes within the UPFA between Premachandra and Silva. Bharatha backed his disciple and incumbent Chairman Prasanna Solangaarachchi. Duminda Silva worked against him and propped up another.

At the time of his death Premachandra was serving as an Adviser on Trade Unions to President Mahinda Rajapaksa. The former Colombo District MP had lost the elections of 2004 and 2010. He had been replaced as Kolonnawa Electoral Organiser by Duminda Silva who had crossed over from the UNP and been elected MP. Premachandra resented this and there was a political turf war between him and Duminda Silva. The Local Authority Poll became a prestige battle.

The campaign was nasty and marred by violence. Bharatha Lakshman with three decades of political experience turned it into a moral crusade against drugs and drug dealers. Popular antipathy towards the “kudukaraya” menace in Kolonnawa struck a responsive chord in the hearts and minds of the voters.

When results were announced it was a great political triumph for Bharatha. His “candidate” had got a record number of 42,322 preferences. His rival’s nominee had only got 5,811 preferences. The voters had overwhelmingly endorsed Bharatha Lakshman’s war against the drug mafia. In the process Premachandra had demonstrated that he was still a political force in the area. Sadly Bharatha Lakshman was not alive to savour his victory. Fate’s moving finger had written his epitaph already.

According to media reports, on Local Election day Bharatha was campaigning in one area and Solangaarachchi in another. Duminda Silva and his group campaigning in another area were reportedly in high spirits after imbibing liquor in an area called “Tamilnaduwatte”. At one stage Duminda and his accomplices went up to a polling booth in close proximity to Solangaarachchi’s residence. Duminda asked Solangaarachchi’s wife Madhu whom she voted for. There was a verbal altercation. When Madhu’s mother-in-law/Solangaarachchi’s mother remonstrated, Duminda Silva abused her also using obscene words. Some allege that both women were roughed up. Duminda also continued to intimidate and threaten Solangaarachchi’s supporters at the spot.

Bharatha Lakshman was informed of what was happening. Since he was close to the spot, Bharatha rushed to the area with his comrades. Duminda heard that Bharatha was coming and left the place. Both convoys met on the road near Walpola junction. Bharatha and Duminda both got down from their vehicles and moved towards each other.

Bharatha asked Duminda why he was trying to bully and frighten women in a cowardly manner. Duminda suddenly hit Bharatha and also kicked him. Taken by surprise Bharatha fell down. At this point Duminda ordered his men to open fire. Realising the seriousness of the situation, Bharatha’s armed “official” bodyguard also opened fire. Bharatha’s comrades who served as his unofficial bodyguards were unarmed. Bharatha and three of his comrades were shot dead. Bharatha was fired upon while he was lying on the ground. His official bodyguard too was injured.

Instead of the perpetrators being arrested, the country was regaled with what appeared to be a massive cover-up exercise. It was reported that Duminda Silva was seriously injured in the shootout. He was admitted to hospital. The then President Mahinda Rajapaksa and his Defence Secretary Gotabaya Rajapaksa visited Duminda in hospital. Special protection was provided. Outsiders were not allowed to go near Duminda. Finally he was escorted amidst great security and was flown to Singapore. It was said that he had sustained many injuries and needed extra medical treatment. Later when Duminda returned to Sri Lanka, he told authorities that he was suffering from partial memory loss and could not remember what had transpired on the day in question.
Duminda Silva was released as a result of a Presidential pardon on 24 June – pic via Daily FT

There was an inordinate delay in effective legal proceedings being instituted. There was a long period of perceived procrastination attributed euphemistically to the law’s delays. Most people however suspected that the close relationship between Duminda Silva and powerful persons in the Mahinda Rajapaksa regime was the stumbling block. The matter dragged on for years and it was widely believed that the aphorism “Justice delayed is justice denied” was being proven true in this instance also.

In an understandable yet naïve bid to secure justice while working within the ruling Rajapaksa establishment, Premachandra’s widow and daughter associated closely with President Mahinda Rajapaksa. Sumana Premachandra like her late husband was appointed as an Advisor to President Rajapaksa.

Daughter Hirunika Premachandra identified herself with the ruling Rajapaksa regime. In 2014 she contested on the UPFA ticket at elections for the Western Provincial Council. She won from Colombo District, topping the preference votes list. In spite of all these efforts the quest of justice for Bharatha seemed as elusive as ever.

Hirunika’s opportunity came when Maithripala Sirisena broke ranks with the Government in November 2014. She seized the moment by hitching her wagon to the star from Polonnaruwa. In 2015 Hirunika contested Parliamentary Elections from Colombo District on the United National Party (UNP) ticket and won. Duminda Silva however was denied nomination for polls by President Maithripala Sirisena.

The advent of a new dispensation under President Maithripala Sirisena and Prime Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe in 2015 saw a fresh breeze blowing in musty judicial corridors. The Attorney-General filed an indictment in courts on March 2015 for the killings of Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra, Dharshana Jayathilake, Mohamed Azmi and Manivel Kumaraswamy and also for inflicting gunshot injuries on Rajapurage Gamini.
The indictment consisted of 17 charges against 13 suspects including Duminda Silva.

Chief Justice Kanagasabapathy Sripavan appointed in May 2015 a three-member bench for Trial-at-Bar proceedings. The bench comprised three High Court Judges, namely Shiran Guneratne, Pathmini Ranawaka and M.C.B.S. Moraes. The Chairman was Shiran Guneratne.

Trial-at-Bar proceedings began on 22 May 2015. The case was heard on a regular basis from 12 September 2015. The names of the 13 accused were Chandana Jagath Kumara, Lanka Rasanjana, Malaka Sameera, Widanagamage Amila, Suranga Premalal, Saman Kumara, Saman Abeywickrema, Rohana Marasinghe, Duminda Silva, Anura Thushara de Mel, Chaminda Ravi Jayanath alias Dematagoda Chaminda, Dissanayake Mudiyanselage Sarath Bandara and Janaka Bandara Galagoda. The last named was tried in absentia as he was absconding. The accused were charged under Sections 296, 140, 146, 147, 486 and 300 of the Penal Code and some clauses of the Firearms Act.

Among the 17 charges were committing and conspiring to commit murder of four individuals, attempted murder, possessing illegal firearms, inflicting gunshot injuries, unlawful assembly and criminal intimidation on or around 8 October 2011. A team of lawyers from the Attorney General’s Department led by Deputy Solicitor General Thusith Mudalige represented the prosecution; 42 witnesses testified in Courts and 126 documents including Government Analysts reports and JMO reports were produced.

Trial-at-Bar proceedings concluded on 14 July 2016.The verdict in the high profile trial was delivered on 8 September 2016. It was a divided verdict with two of three Judges finding five of the accused guilty and acquitting eight others.

One judge however found all 13 accused not guilty. High Court Judge Shiran Guneratne who presided over the Trial-at-Bar exonerated all 13 accused in the murder trial, citing lack of integrity of the witnesses produced by the prosecution and the inability to prove charges beyond reasonable doubt. He said that the benefit of doubt should go to the accused. Fellow High Court Judges Pathmini Ranawaka and M.C.B.S. Moraes differed from Guneratne. This resulted in a two to one majority verdict.

High Court Judge Pathmini N. Ranawaka pronounced the two to one majority verdict in the packed Courthouse with Judge Moraes concurring. Judge Pathmini Ranawaka delivering the majority verdict stated that the prosecution had proved during the trial that the provocative conduct of 11th accused Duminda Silva led to the whole incident.

The Judge further observed that the evidence submitted has proved that Duminda Silva had provided leadership to the unruly behaviour of the mob and therefore he should be held responsible for the whole incident. She further said that the JMO reports had revealed that Duminda Silva was under the influence of liquor at the time of the incident, but deceased Bharatha Premachandra was not so. The verdict also rejected the argument put forward by the defence during trial that Silva had been shot at first by the Premachandra faction.

Courts then acquitted and discharged eight of the accused. They were – Chandana Jagath Kumara, Lanka Rasanjana, Malaka Sameera, Widanagamage Amila, Suranga Premalal, Saman Kumara, Saman Abeywickrema and Rohana Marasinghe. Death sentence was pronounced for five persons. They were – Duminda Silva, Anura Thushara de Mel, Chaminda Ravi Jayanath alias Dematagoda Chaminda, Dissanayake Mudiyanselage Sarath Bandara and Janaka Bandara Galagoda. The 10th accused Janaka Bandara Galagoda was tried in absentia and convicted. In addition to the verdict of execution, the five convicted persons were also fined Rs. 40,000 each.

The Trial-at-Bar High Court ruling was appealed at the Supreme Court. A five-Judge bench of the Supreme Court presided over by the then Chief Justice Priyasath Dep upheld the conviction unanimously. Chief Justice Dep issued a 51-page ruling on 11 October 2018. A summarised news report published on 13 October in the ‘Economy Next’ website is excerpted below:

Sri Lanka’s Supreme Court, which upheld the death penalty of MP Duminda Silva, has also highlighted his criminal activities including the use of an automatic weapon stolen from the Elephant Pass Army Camp. The outgoing Chief Justice Priyasath Dep in his 51-page order noted a string of crimes, including election law violations, committed by Silva and his cohorts culminating in the killing of Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra and three others. The five-Judge bench in its unanimous decision found that the killings came after Silva and his gang beat up rival supporters and voters at the October 2011 local council elections and had even intimidated women.

“Starting from the time the polling commenced and till the time it was drawing to an end, the 11th Accused (Duminda Silva) spent his day, marauding between polling stations with weapons, defying officials discharging their duties, and assaulting and victimising people associated with Solangaarachchi (a rival candidate).The only time they were not seen intimidating people were when the group was having lunch,” the Chief Justice said in his final order before retirement.
Justice Nalin Perera who was appointed Chief Justice on Friday, agreed with Dep’s decision. Others on the bench were justices Buwaneka Aluwihare, Priyantha Jayawardena and Vijith K. Malalgoda.

The Court held that Bharatha Lakshman and three others were shot dead at the behest of Duminda Silva and the weapon used had been stolen from the Elephant Pass Military Camp when it was overrun by the Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam (LTTE) in 2000. It was not mentioned how Silva, a Monitoring MP of the Defence Ministry at the time, came to possess this weapon.

The Supreme Court rejected Silva’s position that he had been shot in the head by Premachandra’s officially-assigned bodyguard and was not responsible for subsequent events, including the killings of Premachandra and the other three people. However, the Court rejected this position on the basis that Silva had been leading the unlawful assembly and triggered the violence by first assaulting Premachandra, whose Personal Security Officer, using the right to private defence, then opened fire and injured Silva.

“Up to the very minute he was shot in his head, the 11th Accused (Duminda Silva) was leading the unlawful assembly. This means that there could only have been a millisecond difference in time between the first shot and the retaliation.

“Causing death using firearms was very much a foreseeable consequence of their criminal enterprise. It is also true that the 11th Accused (Duminda Silva) was a member of that assembly when the transaction—which lasted for a fleeting 60 or more seconds—commenced.

“At the same time, there is no evidence to suggest that, at any time prior to that, the 11th Accused (Duminda Silva) showed a tendency to disassociate himself with the object of the assembly,” the judgement said.

However, the Court acquitted Duminda Silva’s official bodyguard, constable Vithanalage Anura Thushara de Mel who had not used a weapon and had also been against getting into a gun battle. The Supreme Court held that the prosecution had failed to establish that de Mel shared the common objective of the unlawful assembly and that he should therefore be acquitted.

Duminda Silva’s gang had used only one T56 weapon, the one stolen from the Elephant Pass Camp, and 27 spent cartridges found at the crime scene had been fired from it. The Court found that Duminda Silva had systematically moved from one polling centre to the other intimidating voters and had assaulted voters they suspected of voting for the opposition. Women were intimidated and there was a string of violence against people in the area.

This then was what happened in the prosecution and conviction of Duminda Silva for the murder of Bharatha Lakshman Premachandra. Now Duminda has been released due to the clemency of President Rajapaksa. No explanation has been given. There was no reference to the Presidential pardon in the President’s 70-minute televised address to the nation on 25 June.
Under these circumstances, the Bar Association of Sri Lanka BASL has written to the President and requested that the President to the BASL and to the general public:
(a) The basis on which Duminda Silva was selected for the purpose of granting a pardon under Article 34 (1) of the Constitution;
(b) The circumstances which were taken into consideration in the granting of such pardon;
(c) The reasons as to why the case of Duminda Silva stands out from others who are currently sentenced;

(d) Whether a report was called for by His Excellency the President from the Trial Judges as required by the Proviso to Article 34 (1) prior to granting of the pardon to Duminda Silva and if so the contents of the report;
(e) Whether the advice of the Attorney General was called for prior to granting of the pardon to Duminda Silva and if so the contents of such advice;
(f) Whether the recommendation of the Minister of Justice was obtained prior to granting of the pardon to Duminda Silva and if so whether the Minister of Justice made such a recommendation;

“The Bar Association of Sri Lanka maintains that if any one or more considerations stated above, were not satisfied in the current case, the pardon granted to Duminda Silva would be unreasonable and arbitrary and will result in erosion to the rule of law and result in a loss of public confidence in respect of the administration of justice,” it pointed out.
